revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=大祁於賀美神社| 所在地=大阪府羽曳野市大黒496| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|32|32|N|135|37|14|E|scale:10000}}| 祭神=高龗神| 社格=式内社（小）・村社| 別名=山王権現| 本殿=一間社[[流造]]| 例祭=[[10月17日]]}}
'''大祁於賀美神社'''（おおおかみじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。
[[淤加美神|高龗神]]を祀る。
==歴史== [[延喜式神名帳]]に「河内国石川郡 大祁於賀美神社」と記載される神社であるが、[[六国史|国史]]には現れず、明治以前の歴史は不詳である。
かつては現在地よりの東方の丘の頂上にあり、江戸時代までは山王権現と称していた（現在でも氏子は当社を「山王権現」と呼んでいる）。式内・太祁於賀美神社に比定され、村社に列格したが、明治40年11月18日、近隣の村社・八幡神社（現 [[壺井八幡宮]]）に[[神社合祀|合祀]]された。
旧氏子の希望により、昭和30年12月1日、大黒寺の所有地を借用して再建された。
旧社地は当社合祀後に民間に売却され、土砂の採取場となったため、丘はほとんど消失している。
==外部リンク== *[http://www.geocities.jp/engishiki01/kawachi/html/040104-01.html 大祁於賀美神社]（延喜式神社の調査）
{{DEFAULTSORT:おおおかみしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:村社]] [[Category:羽曳野市]]

revision.comment: 追記
revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=大祁於賀美神社| 所在地=大阪府羽曳野市大黒496| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|32|32|N|135|37|14|E|scale:10000}}| 祭神=高龗神| 社格=式内社（小）・村社| 別名=山王権現| 本殿=一間社[[流造]]| 例祭=[[10月17日]]}}
'''大祁於賀美神社'''（おおけおかみじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。
[[淤加美神|高龗神]]を祀る。
==歴史== [[延喜式神名帳]]に「[[河内国]][[石川郡 (大阪府)|石川郡]] 大祁於賀美神社」と記載される神社であるが、[[六国史|国史]]には現れず、江戸時代以前の歴史は不詳である。なお、旧鎮座地・現鎮座地とも旧[[古市郡]]内であるが、これは延喜式の時代の後に郡の境界が変わったためである。
かつては現在地より東方の丘の頂上にあり、江戸時代までは山王権現と称していた（現在でも氏子は当社を「山王権現」と呼んでいる）。[[享和]]元年（[[1801年]]）刊行の『[[河内名所図会]]』に「延喜式石川郡に属す。今山王と称し、此所の本居神とす」とある。明治初年に村社に列格したが、明治40年11月18日、近隣の村社・八幡神社（現 [[壺井八幡宮]]）に[[神社合祀|合祀]]された。
旧氏子の希望により、昭和30年12月1日、天童山大黒寺の所有地を借用して再建された。
旧社地は当社合祀後に民間に売却され、土砂の採取場となったため、丘はほとんど消失している。
==外部リンク== *[http://www.geocities.jp/engishiki01/kawachi/html/040104-01.html 太祁於賀美神社]（延喜式神社の調査） *[http://kamnavi.jp/en/kawati/okiokami.htm 大祁於賀美神社]（神奈備）
{{DEFAULTSORT:おおけおかみしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:村社]] [[Category:羽曳野市]]

かつては現在地より東方の丘の頂上にあり、江戸時代までは山王権現と称していた（現在でも氏子は当社を「山王権現」と呼んでいる）。[[享和]]元年（[[1801年]]）刊行の『[[河内名所図会]]』に「延喜式石川郡に属す。今山王と称し、此所の本居神とす」とある。明治初年に村社に列格したが、明治40年11月18日、近隣の村社・八幡神社（現 [[壺井八幡宮]]）に[[神社合祀|合祀]]された。
旧氏子の希望により、昭和30年12月1日、天童山大黒寺の所有地を借用して再建された。
旧社地は当社合祀後に民間に売却され、土砂の採取場となったため、丘はほとんど消失している。

{{統合文字|祁|ネ|示}} {{神社 | 名称=大祁於賀美神社 | 画像=[[File:Oookami-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 | 所在地=[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]大黒496 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=[[高龗神]] | 社格=[[式内社]]（小）・旧[[村社]] | 別名=[[山王権現]] | 本殿=一間社[[流造]] | 例祭=[[10月17日]] | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=大祁
於賀美神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''大祁於賀美神社'''（たけおかみじんじゃ / おおけおかみじんじゃ / おおおかみじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。 「太祁於賀美神社」とも書き、[[淤加美神|高龗神]]（たかおかみ「水の神」）を祀る。 == 歴史 == [[延喜式神名帳]]に「[[河内国]][[石川郡 (大阪府)|石川郡]] 大祁於賀美神社」と記載される神社であるが、[[六国史|国史]]には現れず、江戸時代以前の歴史は不詳である。なお、旧鎮座地・現鎮座地とも旧[[古市郡]]内であるが、これは延喜式の時代の後に郡の境界が変わったためである。 かつては現在地より東方の丘の頂上にあり、江戸時代までは[[山王権現]]と称していた（現在でも氏子は当社を「山王権現」と呼んでいる）。[[享和]]元年（[[1801年]]）刊行の『[[河内名所図会]]』に「延喜式石川郡に属す。今山王と称し、此所の本居神とす」とある。明治初年に村社に列格したが、明治40年11月18日、近隣の村社・八幡神社（現 [[壺井八幡宮]]）に[[神社合祀|合祀]]された。 旧氏子の希望により、昭和30年（[[1955年]]）12月1日、[[大黒寺 (羽曳野市)|大黒寺]]の所有地を借用して再建された。 旧社地は当社合祀後に民間に売却され、土砂の採取場となったため、丘はほとんど消失している。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Oookami-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおけおかみしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:水神]] [[Category:山王信仰]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]]

{{nihongo|'''Takeokami Shrine'''|大祁於賀美神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Takeokami Shrine'''|大祁於賀美神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Takeokami Shrine'''|大祁於賀美神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Takeokami Shrine'''|大祁於賀美神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Takeokami Shrine'''|大祁於賀美神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Takeokami Shrine'''|大祁於賀美神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Takeokami Shrine'''|大祁於賀美神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Takeokami Shrine'''|大祁於賀美神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Takeokami Shrine'''|大祁於賀美神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Takeokami Shrine'''|大祁於賀美神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Takeokami Shrine'''|大祁於賀美神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Takeokami Shrine'''|大祁於賀美神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

revision.comment: 16 revisions imported
revision.text:
{{統合文字|祁|ネ|示}} {{神社 | 名称=大祁於賀美神社 | 画像=[[File:Oookami-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 | 所在地=[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]大黒496 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=[[高龗神]] | 社格=[[式内社]]（小）・旧[[村社]] | 別名=[[山王権現]] | 本殿=一間社[[流造]] | 例祭=[[10月17日]] | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=大祁
於賀美神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''大祁於賀美神社'''（たけおかみじんじゃ / おおけおかみじんじゃ / おおおかみじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。 「太祁於賀美神社」とも書き、[[淤加美神|高龗神]]（たかおかみ「水の神」）を祀る。 == 歴史 == [[延喜式神名帳]]に「[[河内国]][[石川郡 (大阪府)|石川郡]] 大祁於賀美神社」と記載される神社であるが、[[六国史|国史]]には現れず、江戸時代以前の歴史は不詳である。なお、旧鎮座地・現鎮座地とも旧[[古市郡]]内であるが、これは延喜式の時代の後に郡の境界が変わったためである。 かつては現在地より東方の丘の頂上にあり、江戸時代までは[[山王権現]]と称していた（現在でも氏子は当社を「山王権現」と呼んでいる）。[[享和]]元年（[[1801年]]）刊行の『[[河内名所図会]]』に「延喜式石川郡に属す。今山王と称し、此所の本居神とす」とある。明治初年に村社に列格したが、明治40年11月18日、近隣の村社・八幡神社（現 [[壺井八幡宮]]）に[[神社合祀|合祀]]された。 旧氏子の希望により、昭和30年（[[1955年]]）12月1日、[[大黒寺 (羽曳野市)|大黒寺]]の所有地を借用して再建された。 旧社地は当社合祀後に民間に売却され、土砂の採取場となったため、丘はほとんど消失している。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Oookami-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおけおかみしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:水神]] [[Category:山王信仰]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[大祁於賀美神社]] to [[Takeokami Shrine]] without leaving a redirect: Merging Japanese Wikipedia import with English content
revision.text:

於賀美神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''大祁於賀美神社'''（たけおかみじんじゃ / おおけおかみじんじゃ / おおおかみじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。 「太祁於賀美神社」とも書き、[[淤加美神|高龗神]]（たかおかみ「水の神」）を祀る。 == 歴史 == [[延喜式神名帳]]に「[[河内国]][[石川郡 (大阪府)|石川郡]] 大祁於賀美神社」と記載される神社であるが、[[六国史|国史]]には現れず、江戸時代以前の歴史は不詳である。なお、旧鎮座地・現鎮座地とも旧[[古市郡]]内であるが、これは延喜式の時代の後に郡の境界が変わったためである。 かつては現在地より東方の丘の頂上にあり、江戸時代までは[[山王権現]]と称していた（現在でも氏子は当社を「山王権現」と呼んでいる）。[[享和]]元年（[[1801年]]）刊行の『[[河内名所図会]]』に「延喜式石川郡に属す。今山王と称し、此所の本居神とす」とある。明治初年に村社に列格したが、明治40年11月18日、近隣の村社・八幡神社（現 [[壺井八幡宮]]）に[[神社合祀|合祀]]された。 旧氏子の希望により、昭和30年（[[1955年]]）12月1日、[[大黒寺 (羽曳野市)|大黒寺]]の所有地を借用して再建された。 旧社地は当社合祀後に民間に売却され、土砂の採取場となったため、丘はほとんど消失している。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Oookami-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおけおかみしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:水神]] [[Category:山王信仰]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]]

[[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] {{commons category|Category:Oookami-jinja}} [[JA:大祁於賀美神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11438318}} [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|大祁於賀美神社|version=107260541|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{統合文字|祁|ネ|示}} {{神社 | 名称=大祁於賀美神社 | 画像=[[File:Oookami-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 | 所在地=[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]大黒496 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=[[高龗神]] | 社格=[[式内社]]（小）・旧[[村社]] | 別名=[[山王権現]] | 本殿=一間社[[流造]] | 例祭=[[10月17日]] | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=大祁
於賀美神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''大祁於賀美神社'''（たけおかみじんじゃ / おおけおかみじんじゃ / おおおかみじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。 「太祁於賀美神社」とも書き、[[淤加美神|高龗神]]（たかおかみ「水の神」）を祀る。 == 歴史 == [[延喜式神名帳]]に「[[河内国]][[石川郡 (大阪府)|石川郡]] 大祁於賀美神社」と記載される神社であるが、[[六国史|国史]]には現れず、江戸時代以前の歴史は不詳である。なお、旧鎮座地・現鎮座地とも旧[[古市郡]]内であるが、これは延喜式の時代の後に郡の境界が変わったためである。 かつては現在地より東方の丘の頂上にあり、江戸時代までは[[山王権現]]と称していた（現在でも氏子は当社を「山王権現」と呼んでいる）。[[享和]]元年（[[1801年]]）刊行の『[[河内名所図会]]』に「延喜式石川郡に属す。今山王と称し、此所の本居神とす」とある。明治初年に村社に列格したが、明治40年11月18日、近隣の村社・八幡神社（現 [[壺井八幡宮]]）に[[神社合祀|合祀]]された。 旧氏子の希望により、昭和30年（[[1955年]]）12月1日、[[大黒寺 (羽曳野市)|大黒寺]]の所有地を借用して再建された。 旧社地は当社合祀後に民間に売却され、土砂の採取場となったため、丘はほとんど消失している。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Oookami-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおけおかみしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:水神]] [[Category:山王信仰]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] ==End Japanese==

於賀美神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''大祁於賀美神社'''（たけおかみじんじゃ / おおけおかみじんじゃ / おおおかみじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。 「太祁於賀美神社」とも書き、[[淤加美神|高龗神]]（たかおかみ「水の神」）を祀る。 == 歴史 == [[延喜式神名帳]]に「[[河内国]][[石川郡 (大阪府)|石川郡]] 大祁於賀美神社」と記載される神社であるが、[[六国史|国史]]には現れず、江戸時代以前の歴史は不詳である。なお、旧鎮座地・現鎮座地とも旧[[古市郡]]内であるが、これは延喜式の時代の後に郡の境界が変わったためである。 かつては現在地より東方の丘の頂上にあり、江戸時代までは[[山王権現]]と称していた（現在でも氏子は当社を「山王権現」と呼んでいる）。[[享和]]元年（[[1801年]]）刊行の『[[河内名所図会]]』に「延喜式石川郡に属す。今山王と称し、此所の本居神とす」とある。明治初年に村社に列格したが、明治40年11月18日、近隣の村社・八幡神社（現 [[壺井八幡宮]]）に[[神社合祀|合祀]]された。 旧氏子の希望により、昭和30年（[[1955年]]）12月1日、[[大黒寺 (羽曳野市)|大黒寺]]の所有地を借用して再建された。 旧社地は当社合祀後に民間に売却され、土砂の採取場となったため、丘はほとんど消失している。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Oookami-jinja}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:水神]] [[Category:山王信仰]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] ==End Japanese==

[[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] {{commons category|Oookami-jinja}} [[JA:大祁於賀美神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11438318}} [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|大祁於賀美神社|version=107260541|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{統合文字|祁|ネ|示}} {{神社 | 名称=大祁於賀美神社 | 画像=[[File:Oookami-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 | 所在地=[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]大黒496 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=[[高龗神]] | 社格=[[式内社]]（小）・旧[[村社]] | 別名=[[山王権現]] | 本殿=一間社[[流造]] | 例祭=[[10月17日]] | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=大祁
於賀美神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''大祁於賀美神社'''（たけおかみじんじゃ / おおけおかみじんじゃ / おおおかみじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。 「太祁於賀美神社」とも書き、[[淤加美神|高龗神]]（たかおかみ「水の神」）を祀る。 == 歴史 == [[延喜式神名帳]]に「[[河内国]][[石川郡 (大阪府)|石川郡]] 大祁於賀美神社」と記載される神社であるが、[[六国史|国史]]には現れず、江戸時代以前の歴史は不詳である。なお、旧鎮座地・現鎮座地とも旧[[古市郡]]内であるが、これは延喜式の時代の後に郡の境界が変わったためである。 かつては現在地より東方の丘の頂上にあり、江戸時代までは[[山王権現]]と称していた（現在でも氏子は当社を「山王権現」と呼んでいる）。[[享和]]元年（[[1801年]]）刊行の『[[河内名所図会]]』に「延喜式石川郡に属す。今山王と称し、此所の本居神とす」とある。明治初年に村社に列格したが、明治40年11月18日、近隣の村社・八幡神社（現 [[壺井八幡宮]]）に[[神社合祀|合祀]]された。 旧氏子の希望により、昭和30年（[[1955年]]）12月1日、[[大黒寺 (羽曳野市)|大黒寺]]の所有地を借用して再建された。 旧社地は当社合祀後に民間に売却され、土砂の採取場となったため、丘はほとんど消失している。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Oookami-jinja}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:水神]] [[Category:山王信仰]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]]

{{nihongo|'''Takeokami Shrine'''|大祁於賀美神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

* {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|WD=Q845945}}
* {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|WD=Q134917286}}
* {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}}
* {{ill|Kanpei-sha|lt=Kanpei-sha|simple|Kanpei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160338}}<ref>Kokugakuin University Shrine database</ref>

{{nihongo|'''Takeokami Shrine'''|大祁於賀美神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

name in kana (P1814) == * おおけおかみじんじゃ * おおおかみじんじゃ * たけおかみじんじゃ == Commons category (P373) == * Oookami-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府羽曳野市大黒496', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Oookami-jinja}} [[JA:大祁於賀美神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11438318}} == Japanese content == {{統合文字|祁|ネ|示}} {{神社 | 名称=大祁於賀美神社 | 画像=[[File:Oookami-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 | 所在地={{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|羽曳野市|Q694308}}大黒496 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{jalink|高龗神}} | 社格={{jalink|式内社}}（小）・旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} | 別名={{jalink|山王権現|Q11470054}} | 本殿=一間社{{jalink|流造|Q210684}} | 例祭={{jalink|10月17日|Q2962}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=大祁
於賀美神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''大祁於賀美神社'''（たけおかみじんじゃ / おおけおかみじんじゃ / おおおかみじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|羽曳野市|Q694308}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|式内社}}で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 「太祁於賀美神社」とも書き、{{jalink|淤加美神|Q644201|高龗神}}（たかおかみ「水の神」）を祀る。 === 歴史 === {{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}に「{{jalink|河内国|Q1047150}}{{jalink|石川郡 (大阪府)|Q11586399|石川郡}} 大祁於賀美神社」と記載される神社であるが、{{jalink|六国史|Q1151168|国史}}には現れず、江戸時代以前の歴史は不詳である。なお、旧鎮座地・現鎮座地とも旧{{jalink|古市郡|Q11411239}}内であるが、これは延喜式の時代の後に郡の境界が変わったためである。 かつては現在地より東方の丘の頂上にあり、江戸時代までは{{jalink|山王権現|Q11470054}}と称していた（現在でも氏子は当社を「山王権現」と呼んでいる）。{{jalink|享和|Q960763}}元年（{{jalink|1801年|Q6877}}）刊行の『{{jalink|河内名所図会}}』に「延喜式石川郡に属す。今山王と称し、此所の本居神とす」とある。明治初年に村社に列格したが、明治40年11月18日、近隣の村社・八幡神社（現 {{jalink|壺井八幡宮|Q11429677}}）に{{jalink|神社合祀|Q11590700|合祀}}された。 旧氏子の希望により、昭和30年（{{jalink|1955年|Q18577}}）12月1日、{{jalink|大黒寺 (羽曳野市)|Q17228997|大黒寺}}の所有地を借用して再建された。 旧社地は当社合祀後に民間に売却され、土砂の採取場となったため、丘はほとんど消失している。 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Oookami-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおけおかみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:水神]] [[Category:山王信仰]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]]

category|Category:Oookami-jinja}} [[JA:大祁於賀美神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11438318}} == Japanese content == {{統合文字|祁|ネ|示}} {{神社 | 名称=大祁於賀美神社 | 画像=[[File:Oookami-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 | 所在地={{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|羽曳野市|Q694308}}大黒496 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{jalink|高龗神}} | 社格={{jalink|式内社}}（小）・旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} | 別名={{jalink|山王権現|Q11470054}} | 本殿=一間社{{jalink|流造|Q210684}} | 例祭={{jalink|10月17日|Q2962}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=大祁
於賀美神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''大祁於賀美神社'''（たけおかみじんじゃ / おおけおかみじんじゃ / おおおかみじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|羽曳野市|Q694308}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|式内社}}で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 「太祁於賀美神社」とも書き、{{jalink|淤加美神|Q644201|高龗神}}（たかおかみ「水の神」）を祀る。 === 歴史 === {{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}に「{{jalink|河内国|Q1047150}}{{jalink|石川郡 (大阪府)|Q11586399|石川郡}} 大祁於賀美神社」と記載される神社であるが、{{jalink|六国史|Q1151168|国史}}には現れず、江戸時代以前の歴史は不詳である。なお、旧鎮座地・現鎮座地とも旧{{jalink|古市郡|Q11411239}}内であるが、これは延喜式の時代の後に郡の境界が変わったためである。 かつては現在地より東方の丘の頂上にあり、江戸時代までは{{jalink|山王権現|Q11470054}}と称していた（現在でも氏子は当社を「山王権現」と呼んでいる）。{{jalink|享和|Q960763}}元年（{{jalink|1801年|Q6877}}）刊行の『{{jalink|河内名所図会}}』に「延喜式石川郡に属す。今山王と称し、此所の本居神とす」とある。明治初年に村社に列格したが、明治40年11月18日、近隣の村社・八幡神社（現 {{jalink|壺井八幡宮|Q11429677}}）に{{jalink|神社合祀|Q11590700|合祀}}された。 旧氏子の希望により、昭和30年（{{jalink|1955年|Q18577}}）12月1日、{{jalink|大黒寺 (羽曳野市)|Q17228997|大黒寺}}の所有地を借用して再建された。 旧社地は当社合祀後に民間に売却され、土砂の採取場となったため、丘はほとんど消失している。 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Oookami-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおけおかみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:水神]] [[Category:山王信仰]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:need translation]]

於賀美神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''大祁於賀美神社'''（たけおかみじんじゃ / おおけおかみじんじゃ / おおおかみじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|羽曳野市|Q694308}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|式内社}}で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 「太祁於賀美神社」とも書き、{{jalink|淤加美神|Q644201|高龗神}}（たかおかみ「水の神」）を祀る。 === 歴史 === {{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}に「{{jalink|河内国|Q1047150}}{{jalink|石川郡 (大阪府)|Q11586399|石川郡}} 大祁於賀美神社」と記載される神社であるが、{{jalink|六国史|Q1151168|国史}}には現れず、江戸時代以前の歴史は不詳である。なお、旧鎮座地・現鎮座地とも旧{{jalink|古市郡|Q11411239}}内であるが、これは延喜式の時代の後に郡の境界が変わったためである。 かつては現在地より東方の丘の頂上にあり、江戸時代までは{{jalink|山王権現|Q11470054}}と称していた（現在でも氏子は当社を「山王権現」と呼んでいる）。{{jalink|享和|Q960763}}元年（{{jalink|1801年|Q6877}}）刊行の『{{jalink|河内名所図会}}』に「延喜式石川郡に属す。今山王と称し、此所の本居神とす」とある。明治初年に村社に列格したが、明治40年11月18日、近隣の村社・八幡神社（現 {{jalink|壺井八幡宮|Q11429677}}）に{{jalink|神社合祀|Q11590700|合祀}}された。 旧氏子の希望により、昭和30年（{{jalink|1955年|Q18577}}）12月1日、{{jalink|大黒寺 (羽曳野市)|Q17228997|大黒寺}}の所有地を借用して再建された。 旧社地は当社合祀後に民間に売却され、土砂の採取場となったため、丘はほとんど消失している。 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Oookami-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおけおかみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:水神]] [[Category:山王信仰]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:need translation]]

於賀美神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''大祁於賀美神社'''（たけおかみじんじゃ / おおけおかみじんじゃ / おおおかみじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|羽曳野市|Q694308}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|式内社}}で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 「太祁於賀美神社」とも書き、{{jalink|淤加美神|Q644201|高龗神}}（たかおかみ「水の神」）を祀る。 === 歴史 === {{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}に「{{jalink|河内国|Q1047150}}{{jalink|石川郡 (大阪府)|Q11586399|石川郡}} 大祁於賀美神社」と記載される神社であるが、{{jalink|六国史|Q1151168|国史}}には現れず、江戸時代以前の歴史は不詳である。なお、旧鎮座地・現鎮座地とも旧{{jalink|古市郡|Q11411239}}内であるが、これは延喜式の時代の後に郡の境界が変わったためである。 かつては現在地より東方の丘の頂上にあり、江戸時代までは{{jalink|山王権現|Q11470054}}と称していた（現在でも氏子は当社を「山王権現」と呼んでいる）。{{jalink|享和|Q960763}}元年（{{jalink|1801年|Q6877}}）刊行の『{{jalink|河内名所図会}}』に「延喜式石川郡に属す。今山王と称し、此所の本居神とす」とある。明治初年に村社に列格したが、明治40年11月18日、近隣の村社・八幡神社（現 {{jalink|壺井八幡宮|Q11429677}}）に{{jalink|神社合祀|Q11590700|合祀}}された。 旧氏子の希望により、昭和30年（{{jalink|1955年|Q18577}}）12月1日、{{jalink|大黒寺 (羽曳野市)|Q17228997|大黒寺}}の所有地を借用して再建された。 旧社地は当社合祀後に民間に売却され、土砂の採取場となったため、丘はほとんど消失している。 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Oookami-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおけおかみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:水神]] [[Category:山王信仰]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:need translation]]

於賀美神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''大祁於賀美神社'''（たけおかみじんじゃ / おおけおかみじんじゃ / おおおかみじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|羽曳野市|Q694308}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|式内社}}で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 「太祁於賀美神社」とも書き、{{jalink|淤加美神|Q644201|高龗神}}（たかおかみ「水の神」）を祀る。 === 歴史 === {{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}に「{{jalink|河内国|Q1047150}}{{jalink|石川郡 (大阪府)|Q11586399|石川郡}} 大祁於賀美神社」と記載される神社であるが、{{jalink|六国史|Q1151168|国史}}には現れず、江戸時代以前の歴史は不詳である。なお、旧鎮座地・現鎮座地とも旧{{jalink|古市郡|Q11411239}}内であるが、これは延喜式の時代の後に郡の境界が変わったためである。 かつては現在地より東方の丘の頂上にあり、江戸時代までは{{jalink|山王権現|Q11470054}}と称していた（現在でも氏子は当社を「山王権現」と呼んでいる）。{{jalink|享和|Q960763}}元年（{{jalink|1801年|Q6877}}）刊行の『{{jalink|河内名所図会}}』に「延喜式石川郡に属す。今山王と称し、此所の本居神とす」とある。明治初年に村社に列格したが、明治40年11月18日、近隣の村社・八幡神社（現 {{jalink|壺井八幡宮|Q11429677}}）に{{jalink|神社合祀|Q11590700|合祀}}された。 旧氏子の希望により、昭和30年（{{jalink|1955年|Q18577}}）12月1日、{{jalink|大黒寺 (羽曳野市)|Q17228997|大黒寺}}の所有地を借用して再建された。 旧社地は当社合祀後に民間に売却され、土砂の採取場となったため、丘はほとんど消失している。 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Oookami-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおけおかみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:水神]] [[Category:山王信仰]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:need translation]]

於賀美神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''大祁於賀美神社'''（たけおかみじんじゃ / おおけおかみじんじゃ / おおおかみじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|羽曳野市|Q694308}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|式内社}}で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 「太祁於賀美神社」とも書き、{{jalink|淤加美神|Q644201|高龗神}}（たかおかみ「水の神」）を祀る。 === 歴史 === {{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}に「{{jalink|河内国|Q1047150}}{{jalink|石川郡 (大阪府)|Q11586399|石川郡}} 大祁於賀美神社」と記載される神社であるが、{{jalink|六国史|Q1151168|国史}}には現れず、江戸時代以前の歴史は不詳である。なお、旧鎮座地・現鎮座地とも旧{{jalink|古市郡|Q11411239}}内であるが、これは延喜式の時代の後に郡の境界が変わったためである。 かつては現在地より東方の丘の頂上にあり、江戸時代までは{{jalink|山王権現|Q11470054}}と称していた（現在でも氏子は当社を「山王権現」と呼んでいる）。{{jalink|享和|Q960763}}元年（{{jalink|1801年|Q6877}}）刊行の『{{jalink|河内名所図会}}』に「延喜式石川郡に属す。今山王と称し、此所の本居神とす」とある。明治初年に村社に列格したが、明治40年11月18日、近隣の村社・八幡神社（現 {{jalink|壺井八幡宮|Q11429677}}）に{{jalink|神社合祀|Q11590700|合祀}}された。 旧氏子の希望により、昭和30年（{{jalink|1955年|Q18577}}）12月1日、{{jalink|大黒寺 (羽曳野市)|Q17228997|大黒寺}}の所有地を借用して再建された。 旧社地は当社合祀後に民間に売却され、土砂の採取場となったため、丘はほとんど消失している。 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Oookami-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおけおかみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:水神]] [[Category:山王信仰]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:need translation]]

category|Category:Oookami-jinja}} [[JA:大祁於賀美神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11438318}} == Japanese content == {{Unified character|祁|ネ|示}} {{Shinto shrine | Name = Ōke Okami Shrine | Image = [[File:Oookami-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Habikino City|Q694308}} 496 Daikoku | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Takaokami no Kami}} | Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor) · Former {{jalink|Village shrine|Q134917284}} | Other name = {{jalink|Sannō Gongen|Q11470054}} | Main hall style = Ichiken-sha {{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | Annual festival = {{jalink|October 17|Q2962}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Ōke
Okami Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ōke Okami Shrine''' (Takeokami Jinja / Ōkeokami Jinja / Ōōkami Jinja) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Habikino City|Q694308}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. It is also written as “Take Okami Shrine” (太祁於賀美神社) and enshrines {{jalink|Okami no Kami|Q644201|Takaokami no Kami}} (Takaokami, the “water deity”). === History === This shrine is listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} as “Ōke Okami Shrine, Ishikawa District, {{jalink|Kawachi Province|Q1047150}}{{jalink|Ishikawa District (Osaka Prefecture)|Q11586399|Ishikawa District}},” but it does not appear in the {{jalink|Rikkokushi|Q1151168|National Histories}}, and its history prior to the Edo period is unknown. Both the former and current shrine sites were within the old {{jalink|Furuichi District|Q11411239}}, but this is due to changes in district boundaries after the Engishiki period. Originally, the shrine stood on the summit of a hill to the east of its present location, and until the Edo period it was known as {{jalink|Sannō Gongen|Q11470054}} (even today, parishioners refer to the shrine as “Sannō Gongen”). The 1801 ({{jalink|Kyōwa|Q960763}} 1) publication *{{jalink|Kawachi Meisho Zue}}* states: “Belongs to Ishikawa District in the Engishiki. Now called Sannō, and regarded as the tutelary deity of this place.” In the early Meiji period, it was ranked as a village shrine, but on November 18, 1907 (Meiji 40), it was merged ({{jalink|shrine consolidation|Q11590700|gōshi}}) with the nearby village shrine Hachiman Shrine (now {{jalink|Tsuboi Hachimangū|Q11429677}}). At the request of former parishioners, it was rebuilt on December 1, 1955 ({{jalink|1955|Q18577}}), borrowing land owned by {{jalink|Daikoku-ji (Habikino City)|Q17228997|Daikoku-ji Temple}}. After the shrine’s merger, the former shrine site was sold to private interests and used as a soil extraction site, and the hill has almost entirely disappeared. === References === {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Oookami-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおけおかみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:水神]] [[Category:山王信仰]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:need translation]]

category|Category:Oookami-jinja}} [[JA:大祁於賀美神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11438318}} == Japanese content == {{Unified character|祁|ネ|示}} {{Shinto shrine | Name = Ōke Okami Shrine | Image = [[File:Oookami-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Habikino|Q694308}} Daikoku 496 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Takaokami no Kami}} | Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small) / former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} | Other name = {{jalink|Sannō Gongen|Q11470054}} | Main hall style = One-bay {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | Annual festival = {{jalink|October 17|Q2962}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Ōke
Okami Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ōke Okami Shrine''' (Takeokami Jinja / Ōkeokami Jinja / Ōōkami Jinja) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Habikino|Q694308}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. It is also written as “Take Okami Shrine” (太祁於賀美神社), and enshrines {{jalink|Okami no Kami|Q644201|Takaokami no Kami}} (a water deity). === History === This shrine appears in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} as “Ōke Okami Shrine, Ishikawa District, {{jalink|Kawachi Province|Q1047150}}{{jalink|Ishikawa District (Osaka Prefecture)|Q11586399|Ishikawa District}},” but it is not found in the {{jalink|Rikkokushi|Q1151168|Six National Histories}}, and its history before the Edo period is unknown. Both the former and current shrine sites were within old {{jalink|Furuichi District|Q11411239}}, but that reflects district-boundary changes after the Engishiki period. Originally the shrine stood on a hill summit east of the current site, and until the Edo period it was called {{jalink|Sannō Gongen|Q11470054}} (even now, parishioners call it “Sannō Gongen”). The 1801 ({{jalink|Kyōwa|Q960763}} 1) work ''{{jalink|Kawachi Meisho Zue}}'' says: “Listed under Ishikawa District in the Engishiki. Now called Sannō and revered as the tutelary deity of this place.” In the early Meiji period it became a village shrine, but on November 18, 1907 (Meiji 40), it was merged ({{jalink|shrine consolidation|Q11590700|gōshi}}) into nearby village-shrine Hachiman Shrine (now {{jalink|Tsuboi Hachimangū|Q11429677}}). At the request of former parishioners, it was rebuilt on December 1, 1955 ({{jalink|1955|Q18577}}), borrowing land owned by {{jalink|Daikoku-ji (Habikino)|Q17228997|Daikoku-ji Temple}}. After the merger, the former shrine site was sold and used for soil extraction, and the hill has nearly disappeared. === References === {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Oookami-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおけおかみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:水神]] [[Category:山王信仰]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:need translation]]

category|Category:Oookami-jinja}} [[JA:大祁於賀美神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11438318}} == Japanese content == {{Unified character|祁|ネ|示}} {{Shinto shrine | Name = Ōke Okami Shrine | Image = [[File:Oookami-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Habikino|Q694308}} Daikoku 496 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Takaokami no Kami}} | Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small) / former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} | Other name = {{jalink|Sannō Gongen|Q11470054}} | Main hall style = One-bay {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | Annual festival = {{jalink|October 17|Q2962}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Ōke
Okami Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ōke Okami Shrine''' (Takeokami Jinja / Ōkeokami Jinja / Ōōkami Jinja) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Habikino|Q694308}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. It is also written as “Take Okami Shrine” (太祁於賀美神社), and enshrines {{jalink|Okami no Kami|Q644201|Takaokami no Kami}} (a water deity). === History === This shrine appears in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} as “Ōke Okami Shrine, Ishikawa District, {{jalink|Kawachi Province|Q1047150}}{{jalink|Ishikawa District (Osaka Prefecture)|Q11586399|Ishikawa District}},” but it is not found in the {{jalink|Rikkokushi|Q1151168|Six National Histories}}, and its history before the Edo period is unknown. Both the former and current shrine sites were within old {{jalink|Furuichi District|Q11411239}}, but that reflects district-boundary changes after the Engishiki period. Originally the shrine stood on a hill summit east of the current site, and until the Edo period it was called {{jalink|Sannō Gongen|Q11470054}} (even now, parishioners call it “Sannō Gongen”). The 1801 ({{jalink|Kyōwa|Q960763}} 1) work ''{{jalink|Kawachi Meisho Zue}}'' says: “Listed under Ishikawa District in the Engishiki. Now called Sannō and revered as the tutelary deity of this place.” In the early Meiji period it became a village shrine, but on November 18, 1907 (Meiji 40), it was merged ({{jalink|shrine consolidation|Q11590700|gōshi}}) into nearby village-shrine Hachiman Shrine (now {{jalink|Tsuboi Hachimangū|Q11429677}}). At the request of former parishioners, it was rebuilt on December 1, 1955 ({{jalink|1955|Q18577}}), borrowing land owned by {{jalink|Daikoku-ji (Habikino)|Q17228997|Daikoku-ji Temple}}. After the merger, the former shrine site was sold and used for soil extraction, and the hill has nearly disappeared. === References === {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Oookami-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおけおかみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:水神]] [[Category:山王信仰]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:need translation]]

category|Category:Oookami-jinja}} [[JA:大祁於賀美神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11438318}} == Japanese content == {{Unified character|祁|ネ|示}} {{Shinto shrine | Name = Ōke Okami Shrine | Image = [[File:Oookami-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Habikino|Q694308}} Daikoku 496 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Takaokami no Kami}} | Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small) / former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} | Other name = {{jalink|Sannō Gongen|Q11470054}} | Main hall style = One-bay {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | Annual festival = {{jalink|October 17|Q2962}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Ōke
Okami Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ōke Okami Shrine''' (Takeokami Jinja / Ōkeokami Jinja / Ōōkami Jinja) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Habikino|Q694308}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. It is also written as “Take Okami Shrine” (太祁於賀美神社), and enshrines {{jalink|Okami no Kami|Q644201|Takaokami no Kami}} (a water deity). === History === This shrine appears in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} as “Ōke Okami Shrine, Ishikawa District, {{jalink|Kawachi Province|Q1047150}}{{jalink|Ishikawa District (Osaka Prefecture)|Q11586399|Ishikawa District}},” but it is not found in the {{jalink|Rikkokushi|Q1151168|Six National Histories}}, and its history before the Edo period is unknown. Both the former and current shrine sites were within old {{jalink|Furuichi District|Q11411239}}, but that reflects district-boundary changes after the Engishiki period. Originally the shrine stood on a hill summit east of the current site, and until the Edo period it was called {{jalink|Sannō Gongen|Q11470054}} (even now, parishioners call it “Sannō Gongen”). The 1801 ({{jalink|Kyōwa|Q960763}} 1) work ''{{jalink|Kawachi Meisho Zue}}'' says: “Listed under Ishikawa District in the Engishiki. Now called Sannō and revered as the tutelary deity of this place.” In the early Meiji period it became a village shrine, but on November 18, 1907 (Meiji 40), it was merged ({{jalink|shrine consolidation|Q11590700|gōshi}}) into nearby village-shrine Hachiman Shrine (now {{jalink|Tsuboi Hachimangū|Q11429677}}). At the request of former parishioners, it was rebuilt on December 1, 1955 ({{jalink|1955|Q18577}}), borrowing land owned by {{jalink|Daikoku-ji (Habikino)|Q17228997|Daikoku-ji Temple}}. After the merger, the former shrine site was sold and used for soil extraction, and the hill has nearly disappeared. === References === {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Oookami-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおけおかみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:水神]] [[Category:山王信仰]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:need translation]]

category|Category:Oookami-jinja}} [[JA:大祁於賀美神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11438318}} == Japanese content == {{Unified character|祁|ネ|示}} {{Shinto shrine | Name = Ōke Okami Shrine | Image = [[File:Oookami-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Habikino|Q694308}} Daikoku 496 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Takaokami no Kami}} | Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small) / former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} | Other name = {{jalink|Sannō Gongen|Q11470054}} | Main hall style = One-bay {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | Annual festival = {{jalink|October 17|Q2962}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Ōke
Okami Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ōke Okami Shrine''' (Takeokami Jinja / Ōkeokami Jinja / Ōōkami Jinja) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Habikino|Q694308}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. It is also written as “Take Okami Shrine” (太祁於賀美神社), and enshrines {{jalink|Okami no Kami|Q644201|Takaokami no Kami}} (a water deity). === History === This shrine appears in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} as “Ōke Okami Shrine, Ishikawa District, {{jalink|Kawachi Province|Q1047150}}{{jalink|Ishikawa District (Osaka Prefecture)|Q11586399|Ishikawa District}},” but it is not found in the {{jalink|Rikkokushi|Q1151168|Six National Histories}}, and its history before the Edo period is unknown. Both the former and current shrine sites were within old {{jalink|Furuichi District|Q11411239}}, but that reflects district-boundary changes after the Engishiki period. Originally the shrine stood on a hill summit east of the current site, and until the Edo period it was called {{jalink|Sannō Gongen|Q11470054}} (even now, parishioners call it “Sannō Gongen”). The 1801 ({{jalink|Kyōwa|Q960763}} 1) work ''{{jalink|Kawachi Meisho Zue}}'' says: “Listed under Ishikawa District in the Engishiki. Now called Sannō and revered as the tutelary deity of this place.” In the early Meiji period it became a village shrine, but on November 18, 1907 (Meiji 40), it was merged ({{jalink|shrine consolidation|Q11590700|gōshi}}) into nearby village-shrine Hachiman Shrine (now {{jalink|Tsuboi Hachimangū|Q11429677}}). At the request of former parishioners, it was rebuilt on December 1, 1955 ({{jalink|1955|Q18577}}), borrowing land owned by {{jalink|Daikoku-ji (Habikino)|Q17228997|Daikoku-ji Temple}}. After the merger, the former shrine site was sold and used for soil extraction, and the hill has nearly disappeared. === References === {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Oookami-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおけおかみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:水神]] [[Category:山王信仰]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:need translation]]

category|Category:Oookami-jinja}} [[JA:大祁於賀美神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11438318}} == Japanese content == {{Unified character|祁|ネ|示}} {{Shinto shrine | Name = Ōke Okami Shrine | Image = [[File:Oookami-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Habikino|Q694308}} Daikoku 496 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Takaokami no Kami}} | Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small) / former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} | Other name = {{jalink|Sannō Gongen|Q11470054}} | Main hall style = One-bay {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | Annual festival = {{jalink|October 17|Q2962}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Ōke
Okami Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ōke Okami Shrine''' (Takeokami Jinja / Ōkeokami Jinja / Ōōkami Jinja) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Habikino|Q694308}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. It is also written as “Take Okami Shrine” (太祁於賀美神社), and enshrines {{jalink|Okami no Kami|Q644201|Takaokami no Kami}} (a water deity). === History === This shrine appears in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} as “Ōke Okami Shrine, Ishikawa District, {{jalink|Kawachi Province|Q1047150}}{{jalink|Ishikawa District (Osaka Prefecture)|Q11586399|Ishikawa District}},” but it is not found in the {{jalink|Rikkokushi|Q1151168|Six National Histories}}, and its history before the Edo period is unknown. Both the former and current shrine sites were within old {{jalink|Furuichi District|Q11411239}}, but that reflects district-boundary changes after the Engishiki period. Originally the shrine stood on a hill summit east of the current site, and until the Edo period it was called {{jalink|Sannō Gongen|Q11470054}} (even now, parishioners call it “Sannō Gongen”). The 1801 ({{jalink|Kyōwa|Q960763}} 1) work ''{{jalink|Kawachi Meisho Zue}}'' says: “Listed under Ishikawa District in the Engishiki. Now called Sannō and revered as the tutelary deity of this place.” In the early Meiji period it became a village shrine, but on November 18, 1907 (Meiji 40), it was merged ({{jalink|shrine consolidation|Q11590700|gōshi}}) into nearby village-shrine Hachiman Shrine (now {{jalink|Tsuboi Hachimangū|Q11429677}}). At the request of former parishioners, it was rebuilt on December 1, 1955 ({{jalink|1955|Q18577}}), borrowing land owned by {{jalink|Daikoku-ji (Habikino)|Q17228997|Daikoku-ji Temple}}. After the merger, the former shrine site was sold and used for soil extraction, and the hill has nearly disappeared. === References === {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Oookami-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおけおかみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:水神]] [[Category:山王信仰]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]]

category|Category:Oookami-jinja}} [[JA:大祁於賀美神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11438318}} == Japanese content == {{Unified character|祁|ネ|示}} {{Shinto shrine | Name = Ōke Okami Shrine | Image = [[File:Oookami-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Habikino|Q694308}} Daikoku 496 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Takaokami no Kami}} | Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small) / former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} | Other name = {{jalink|Sannō Gongen|Q11470054}} | Main hall style = One-bay {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | Annual festival = {{jalink|October 17|Q2962}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Ōke
Okami Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ōke Okami Shrine''' (Takeokami Jinja / Ōkeokami Jinja / Ōōkami Jinja) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Habikino|Q694308}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. It is also written as “Take Okami Shrine” (太祁於賀美神社), and enshrines {{jalink|Okami no Kami|Q644201|Takaokami no Kami}} (a water deity). === History === This shrine appears in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} as “Ōke Okami Shrine, Ishikawa District, {{jalink|Kawachi Province|Q1047150}}{{jalink|Ishikawa District (Osaka Prefecture)|Q11586399|Ishikawa District}},” but it is not found in the {{jalink|Rikkokushi|Q1151168|Six National Histories}}, and its history before the Edo period is unknown. Both the former and current shrine sites were within old {{jalink|Furuichi District|Q11411239}}, but that reflects district-boundary changes after the Engishiki period. Originally the shrine stood on a hill summit east of the current site, and until the Edo period it was called {{jalink|Sannō Gongen|Q11470054}} (even now, parishioners call it “Sannō Gongen”). The 1801 ({{jalink|Kyōwa|Q960763}} 1) work ''{{jalink|Kawachi Meisho Zue}}'' says: “Listed under Ishikawa District in the Engishiki. Now called Sannō and revered as the tutelary deity of this place.” In the early Meiji period it became a village shrine, but on November 18, 1907 (Meiji 40), it was merged ({{jalink|shrine consolidation|Q11590700|gōshi}}) into nearby village-shrine Hachiman Shrine (now {{jalink|Tsuboi Hachimangū|Q11429677}}). At the request of former parishioners, it was rebuilt on December 1, 1955 ({{jalink|1955|Q18577}}), borrowing land owned by {{jalink|Daikoku-ji (Habikino)|Q17228997|Daikoku-ji Temple}}. After the merger, the former shrine site was sold and used for soil extraction, and the hill has nearly disappeared. === References === {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Oookami-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおけおかみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:水神]] [[Category:山王信仰]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]][[Category:qq]]

category|Category:Oookami-jinja}} [[JA:大祁於賀美神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11438318}} == Japanese content == {{Unified character|祁|ネ|示}} {{Shinto shrine | Name = Ōke Okami Shrine | Image = [[File:Oookami-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Habikino|Q694308}} Daikoku 496 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Takaokami no Kami}} | Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small) / former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} | Other name = {{jalink|Sannō Gongen|Q11470054}} | Main hall style = One-bay {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | Annual festival = {{jalink|October 17|Q2962}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Ōke
Okami Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ōke Okami Shrine''' (Takeokami Jinja / Ōkeokami Jinja / Ōōkami Jinja) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Habikino|Q694308}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. It is also written as “Take Okami Shrine” (太祁於賀美神社), and enshrines {{jalink|Okami no Kami|Q644201|Takaokami no Kami}} (a water deity). === History === This shrine appears in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} as “Ōke Okami Shrine, Ishikawa District, {{jalink|Kawachi Province|Q1047150}}{{jalink|Ishikawa District (Osaka Prefecture)|Q11586399|Ishikawa District}},” but it is not found in the {{jalink|Rikkokushi|Q1151168|Six National Histories}}, and its history before the Edo period is unknown. Both the former and current shrine sites were within old {{jalink|Furuichi District|Q11411239}}, but that reflects district-boundary changes after the Engishiki period. Originally the shrine stood on a hill summit east of the current site, and until the Edo period it was called {{jalink|Sannō Gongen|Q11470054}} (even now, parishioners call it “Sannō Gongen”). The 1801 ({{jalink|Kyōwa|Q960763}} 1) work ''{{jalink|Kawachi Meisho Zue}}'' says: “Listed under Ishikawa District in the Engishiki. Now called Sannō and revered as the tutelary deity of this place.” In the early Meiji period it became a village shrine, but on November 18, 1907 (Meiji 40), it was merged ({{jalink|shrine consolidation|Q11590700|gōshi}}) into nearby village-shrine Hachiman Shrine (now {{jalink|Tsuboi Hachimangū|Q11429677}}). At the request of former parishioners, it was rebuilt on December 1, 1955 ({{jalink|1955|Q18577}}), borrowing land owned by {{jalink|Daikoku-ji (Habikino)|Q17228997|Daikoku-ji Temple}}. After the merger, the former shrine site was sold and used for soil extraction, and the hill has nearly disappeared. === References === {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Oookami-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおけおかみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:水神]] [[Category:山王信仰]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

category|Category:Oookami-jinja}} [[JA:大祁於賀美神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11438318}} == Japanese content == {{Unified character|祁|ネ|示}} {{Shinto shrine | Name = Ōke Okami Shrine | Image = [[File:Oookami-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Habikino|Q694308}} Daikoku 496 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Takaokami no Kami}} | Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small) / former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} | Other name = {{jalink|Sannō Gongen|Q11470054}} | Main hall style = One-bay {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | Annual festival = {{jalink|October 17|Q2962}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Ōke
Okami Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ōke Okami Shrine''' (Takeokami Jinja / Ōkeokami Jinja / Ōōkami Jinja) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Habikino|Q694308}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. It is also written as “Take Okami Shrine” (太祁於賀美神社), and enshrines {{jalink|Okami no Kami|Q644201|Takaokami no Kami}} (a water deity). === History === This shrine appears in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} as “Ōke Okami Shrine, Ishikawa District, {{jalink|Kawachi Province|Q1047150}}{{jalink|Ishikawa District (Osaka Prefecture)|Q11586399|Ishikawa District}},” but it is not found in the {{jalink|Rikkokushi|Q1151168|Six National Histories}}, and its history before the Edo period is unknown. Both the former and current shrine sites were within old {{jalink|Furuichi District|Q11411239}}, but that reflects district-boundary changes after the Engishiki period. Originally the shrine stood on a hill summit east of the current site, and until the Edo period it was called {{jalink|Sannō Gongen|Q11470054}} (even now, parishioners call it “Sannō Gongen”). The 1801 ({{jalink|Kyōwa|Q960763}} 1) work ''{{jalink|Kawachi Meisho Zue}}'' says: “Listed under Ishikawa District in the Engishiki. Now called Sannō and revered as the tutelary deity of this place.” In the early Meiji period it became a village shrine, but on November 18, 1907 (Meiji 40), it was merged ({{jalink|shrine consolidation|Q11590700|gōshi}}) into nearby village-shrine Hachiman Shrine (now {{jalink|Tsuboi Hachimangū|Q11429677}}). At the request of former parishioners, it was rebuilt on December 1, 1955 ({{jalink|1955|Q18577}}), borrowing land owned by {{jalink|Daikoku-ji (Habikino)|Q17228997|Daikoku-ji Temple}}. After the merger, the former shrine site was sold and used for soil extraction, and the hill has nearly disappeared. === References === {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Oookami-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおけおかみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:水神]] [[Category:山王信仰]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

Now called Sannō and revered as the tutelary deity of this place.” In the early Meiji period it became a village shrine, but on November 18, 1907 (Meiji 40), it was merged ({{ill|lt=shrine consolidation|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|lt=gōshi}}) into nearby village-shrine Hachiman Shrine (now {{ill|lt=Tsuboi Hachimangū|Q11429677|WD=Q11429677}}). At the request of former parishioners, it was rebuilt on December 1, 1955 ({{ill|lt=1955|Q18577|WD=Q18577}}), borrowing land owned by {{ill|lt=Daikoku-ji (Habikino)|Q17228997|WD=Q17228997|lt=Daikoku-ji Temple}}. After the merger, the former shrine site was sold and used for soil extraction, and the hill has nearly disappeared. === References === {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Oookami-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおけおかみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:水神]] [[Category:山王信仰]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

category|Category:Oookami-jinja}} [[JA:大祁於賀美神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11438318}} == Japanese content == {{Unified character|祁|ネ|示}} {{Shinto shrine | Name = Ōke Okami Shrine | Image = [[File:Oookami-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308}} Daikoku 496 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami no Kami|UNKNOWN}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | Other name = {{ill|lt=Sannō Gongen|Q11470054|WD=Q11470054}} | Main hall style = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | Annual festival = {{ill|lt=October 17|Q2962|WD=Q2962}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Ōke
Okami Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ōke Okami Shrine''' (Takeokami Jinja / Ōkeokami Jinja / Ōōkami Jinja) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. It is also written as “Take Okami Shrine” (太祁於賀美神社), and enshrines {{ill|lt=Okami no Kami|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Takaokami no Kami}} (a water deity). === History === This shrine appears in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} as “Ōke Okami Shrine, Ishikawa District, {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150}}{{ill|lt=Ishikawa District (Osaka Prefecture)|Q11586399|WD=Q11586399|lt=Ishikawa District}},” but it is not found in the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=Six National Histories}}, and its history before the Edo period is unknown. Both the former and current shrine sites were within old {{ill|lt=Furuichi District|Q11411239|WD=Q11411239}}, but that reflects district-boundary changes after the Engishiki period. Originally the shrine stood on a hill summit east of the current site, and until the Edo period it was called {{ill|lt=Sannō Gongen|Q11470054|WD=Q11470054}} (even now, parishioners call it “Sannō Gongen”). The 1801 ({{ill|lt=Kyōwa|Q960763|WD=Q960763}} 1) work ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawachi Meisho Zue|UNKNOWN}}'' says: “Listed under Ishikawa District in the Engishiki. Now called Sannō and revered as the tutelary deity of this place.” In the early Meiji period it became a village shrine, but on November 18, 1907 (Meiji 40), it was merged ({{ill|lt=shrine consolidation|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|lt=gōshi}}) into nearby village-shrine Hachiman Shrine (now {{ill|lt=Tsuboi Hachimangū|Q11429677|WD=Q11429677}}). At the request of former parishioners, it was rebuilt on December 1, 1955 ({{ill|lt=1955|Q18577|WD=Q18577}}), borrowing land owned by {{ill|lt=Daikoku-ji (Habikino)|Q17228997|WD=Q17228997|lt=Daikoku-ji Temple}}. After the merger, the former shrine site was sold and used for soil extraction, and the hill has nearly disappeared. === References === {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Oookami-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおけおかみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:水神]] [[Category:山王信仰]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

name in kana (P1814) == * おおけおかみじんじゃ * おおおかみじんじゃ * たけおかみじんじゃ == Commons category (P373) == * Oookami-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府羽曳野市大黒496', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Oookami-jinja}} [[JA:大祁於賀美神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11438318}} == Japanese content == {{Unified character|祁|ネ|示}} {{Shinto shrine | Name = Ōke Okami Shrine | Image = [[File:Oookami-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308}} Daikoku 496 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami no Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | Other name = {{ill|lt=Sannō Gongen|Q11470054|WD=Q11470054}} | Main hall style = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | Annual festival = {{ill|lt=October 17|Q2962|WD=Q2962}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Ōke
Okami Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ōke Okami Shrine''' (Takeokami Jinja / Ōkeokami Jinja / Ōōkami Jinja) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. It is also written as “Take Okami Shrine” (太祁於賀美神社), and enshrines {{ill|lt=Okami no Kami|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Takaokami no Kami}} (a water deity). === History === This shrine appears in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} as “Ōke Okami Shrine, Ishikawa District, {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150}}{{ill|lt=Ishikawa District (Osaka Prefecture)|Q11586399|WD=Q11586399|lt=Ishikawa District}},” but it is not found in the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=Six National Histories}}, and its history before the Edo period is unknown. Both the former and current shrine sites were within old {{ill|lt=Furuichi District|Q11411239|WD=Q11411239}}, but that reflects district-boundary changes after the Engishiki period. Originally the shrine stood on a hill summit east of the current site, and until the Edo period it was called {{ill|lt=Sannō Gongen|Q11470054|WD=Q11470054}} (even now, parishioners call it “Sannō Gongen”). The 1801 ({{ill|lt=Kyōwa|Q960763|WD=Q960763}} 1) work ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawachi Meisho Zue|1=UNKNOWN}}'' says: “Listed under Ishikawa District in the Engishiki. Now called Sannō and revered as the tutelary deity of this place.” In the early Meiji period it became a village shrine, but on November 18, 1907 (Meiji 40), it was merged ({{ill|lt=shrine consolidation|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|lt=gōshi}}) into nearby village-shrine Hachiman Shrine (now {{ill|lt=Tsuboi Hachimangū|Q11429677|WD=Q11429677}}). At the request of former parishioners, it was rebuilt on December 1, 1955 ({{ill|lt=1955|Q18577|WD=Q18577}}), borrowing land owned by {{ill|lt=Daikoku-ji (Habikino)|Q17228997|WD=Q17228997|lt=Daikoku-ji Temple}}. After the merger, the former shrine site was sold and used for soil extraction, and the hill has nearly disappeared. === References === {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Oookami-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおけおかみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:水神]] [[Category:山王信仰]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

category|Category:Oookami-jinja}} [[JA:大祁於賀美神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11438318}} == Japanese content == {{Unified character|祁|ネ|示}} {{Shinto shrine | Name = Ōke Okami Shrine | Image = [[File:Oookami-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308}} Daikoku 496 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami no Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | Other name = {{ill|lt=Sannō Gongen|Q11470054|WD=Q11470054}} | Main hall style = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | Annual festival = {{ill|lt=October 17|Q2962|WD=Q2962}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Ōke
Okami Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ōke Okami Shrine''' (Takeokami Jinja / Ōkeokami Jinja / Ōōkami Jinja) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. It is also written as “Take Okami Shrine” (太祁於賀美神社), and enshrines {{ill|lt=Okami no Kami|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Takaokami no Kami}} (a water deity). === History === This shrine appears in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} as “Ōke Okami Shrine, Ishikawa District, {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150}}{{ill|lt=Ishikawa District (Osaka Prefecture)|Q11586399|WD=Q11586399|lt=Ishikawa District}},” but it is not found in the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=Six National Histories}}, and its history before the Edo period is unknown. Both the former and current shrine sites were within old {{ill|lt=Furuichi District|Q11411239|WD=Q11411239}}, but that reflects district-boundary changes after the Engishiki period. Originally the shrine stood on a hill summit east of the current site, and until the Edo period it was called {{ill|lt=Sannō Gongen|Q11470054|WD=Q11470054}} (even now, parishioners call it “Sannō Gongen”). The 1801 ({{ill|lt=Kyōwa|Q960763|WD=Q960763}} 1) work ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawachi Meisho Zue|1=UNKNOWN}}'' says: “Listed under Ishikawa District in the Engishiki. Now called Sannō and revered as the tutelary deity of this place.” In the early Meiji period it became a village shrine, but on November 18, 1907 (Meiji 40), it was merged ({{ill|lt=shrine consolidation|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|lt=gōshi}}) into nearby village-shrine Hachiman Shrine (now {{ill|lt=Tsuboi Hachimangū|Q11429677|WD=Q11429677}}). At the request of former parishioners, it was rebuilt on December 1, 1955 ({{ill|lt=1955|Q18577|WD=Q18577}}), borrowing land owned by {{ill|lt=Daikoku-ji (Habikino)|Q17228997|WD=Q17228997|lt=Daikoku-ji Temple}}. After the merger, the former shrine site was sold and used for soil extraction, and the hill has nearly disappeared. === References === {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Oookami-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおけおかみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:水神]] [[Category:山王信仰]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

name in kana (P1814) == * おおけおかみじんじゃ * おおおかみじんじゃ * たけおかみじんじゃ == Commons category (P373) == * Oookami-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府羽曳野市大黒496', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Oookami-jinja}} [[JA:大祁於賀美神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11438318}} == Japanese content == {{Unified character|祁|ネ|示}} {{Shinto shrine | Name = Ōke Okami Shrine | Image = [[File:Oookami-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308}} Daikoku 496 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami no Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | Other name = {{ill|lt=Sannō Gongen|Q11470054|WD=Q11470054}} | Main hall style = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | Annual festival = {{ill|lt=October 17|Q2962|WD=Q2962}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Ōke
Okami Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ōke Okami Shrine''' (Takeokami Jinja / Ōkeokami Jinja / Ōōkami Jinja) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. It is also written as “Take Okami Shrine” (太祁於賀美神社), and enshrines {{ill|lt=Okami no Kami|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Takaokami no Kami}} (a water deity). === History === This shrine appears in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} as “Ōke Okami Shrine, Ishikawa District, {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150}}{{ill|lt=Ishikawa District (Osaka Prefecture)|Q11586399|WD=Q11586399|lt=Ishikawa District}},” but it is not found in the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=Six National Histories}}, and its history before the Edo period is unknown. Both the former and current shrine sites were within old {{ill|lt=Furuichi District|Q11411239|WD=Q11411239}}, but that reflects district-boundary changes after the Engishiki period. Originally the shrine stood on a hill summit east of the current site, and until the Edo period it was called {{ill|lt=Sannō Gongen|Q11470054|WD=Q11470054}} (even now, parishioners call it “Sannō Gongen”). The 1801 ({{ill|lt=Kyōwa|Q960763|WD=Q960763}} 1) work ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawachi Meisho Zue|1=UNKNOWN}}'' says: “Listed under Ishikawa District in the Engishiki. Now called Sannō and revered as the tutelary deity of this place.” In the early Meiji period it became a village shrine, but on November 18, 1907 (Meiji 40), it was merged ({{ill|lt=shrine consolidation|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|lt=gōshi}}) into nearby village-shrine Hachiman Shrine (now {{ill|lt=Tsuboi Hachimangū|Q11429677|WD=Q11429677}}). At the request of former parishioners, it was rebuilt on December 1, 1955 ({{ill|lt=1955|Q18577|WD=Q18577}}), borrowing land owned by {{ill|lt=Daikoku-ji (Habikino)|Q17228997|WD=Q17228997|lt=Daikoku-ji Temple}}. After the merger, the former shrine site was sold and used for soil extraction, and the hill has nearly disappeared. === References === {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Oookami-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおけおかみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:水神]] [[Category:山王信仰]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

category|Category:Oookami-jinja}} [[JA:大祁於賀美神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11438318}} == Japanese content == {{Unified character|祁|ネ|示}} {{Shinto shrine | Name = Ōke Okami Shrine | Image = [[File:Oookami-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}} Daikoku 496 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami no Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | Other name = {{ill|lt=Sannō Gongen|Q11470054|WD=Q11470054|1=Sannō Gongen}} | Main hall style = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Annual festival = {{ill|lt=October 17|Q2962|WD=Q2962|1=October 17}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Ōke
Okami Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ōke Okami Shrine''' (Takeokami Jinja / Ōkeokami Jinja / Ōōkami Jinja) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It is also written as “Take Okami Shrine” (太祁於賀美神社), and enshrines {{ill|lt=Okami no Kami|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Takaokami no Kami|1=Kuraokami}} (a water deity). === History === This shrine appears in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as “Ōke Okami Shrine, Ishikawa District, {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}}{{ill|lt=Ishikawa District (Osaka Prefecture)|Q11586399|WD=Q11586399|lt=Ishikawa District|1=Ishikawa district}},” but it is not found in the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=Six National Histories|1=Rikkokushi}}, and its history before the Edo period is unknown. Both the former and current shrine sites were within old {{ill|lt=Furuichi District|Q11411239|WD=Q11411239|1=Furuichi district}}, but that reflects district-boundary changes after the Engishiki period. Originally the shrine stood on a hill summit east of the current site, and until the Edo period it was called {{ill|lt=Sannō Gongen|Q11470054|WD=Q11470054|1=Sannō Gongen}} (even now, parishioners call it “Sannō Gongen”). The 1801 ({{ill|lt=Kyōwa|Q960763|WD=Q960763|1=Kyōwa}} 1) work ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawachi Meisho Zue|1=UNKNOWN}}'' says: “Listed under Ishikawa District in the Engishiki. Now called Sannō and revered as the tutelary deity of this place.” In the early Meiji period it became a village shrine, but on November 18, 1907 (Meiji 40), it was merged ({{ill|lt=shrine consolidation|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|lt=gōshi|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}) into nearby village-shrine Hachiman Shrine (now {{ill|lt=Tsuboi Hachimangū|Q11429677|WD=Q11429677|1=Tsuboi Hachimangū}}). At the request of former parishioners, it was rebuilt on December 1, 1955 ({{ill|lt=1955|Q18577|WD=Q18577|1=1955}}), borrowing land owned by {{ill|lt=Daikoku-ji (Habikino)|Q17228997|WD=Q17228997|lt=Daikoku-ji Temple|1=Daikoku-ji Temple}}. After the merger, the former shrine site was sold and used for soil extraction, and the hill has nearly disappeared. === References === {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Oookami-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおけおかみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:水神]] [[Category:山王信仰]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]
