category|Category:Oookami-jinja}} [[JA:大祁於賀美神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11438318}} == Japanese content == {{Unified character|祁|ネ|示}} {{Shinto shrine | Name = Ōke Okami Shrine | Image = [[File:Oookami-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}} Daikoku 496 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami no Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | Other name = {{ill|lt=Sannō Gongen|Q11470054|WD=Q11470054|1=Sannō Gongen}} | Main hall style = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Annual festival = {{ill|lt=October 17|Q2962|WD=Q2962|1=October 17}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Ōke
Okami Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ōke Okami Shrine''' (Takeokami Jinja / Ōkeokami Jinja / Ōōkami Jinja) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It is also written as “Take Okami Shrine” (太祁於賀美神社), and enshrines {{ill|lt=Okami no Kami|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Takaokami no Kami|1=Kuraokami}} (a water deity). === History === This shrine appears in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as “Ōke Okami Shrine, Ishikawa District, {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}}{{ill|lt=Ishikawa District (Osaka Prefecture)|Q11586399|WD=Q11586399|lt=Ishikawa District|1=Ishikawa district}},” but it is not found in the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=Six National Histories|1=Rikkokushi}}, and its history before the Edo period is unknown. Both the former and current shrine sites were within old {{ill|lt=Furuichi District|Q11411239|WD=Q11411239|1=Furuichi district}}, but that reflects district-boundary changes after the Engishiki period. Originally the shrine stood on a hill summit east of the current site, and until the Edo period it was called {{ill|lt=Sannō Gongen|Q11470054|WD=Q11470054|1=Sannō Gongen}} (even now, parishioners call it “Sannō Gongen”). The 1801 ({{ill|lt=Kyōwa|Q960763|WD=Q960763|1=Kyōwa}} 1) work ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawachi Meisho Zue|1=UNKNOWN}}'' says: “Listed under Ishikawa District in the Engishiki. Now called Sannō and revered as the tutelary deity of this place.” In the early Meiji period it became a village shrine, but on November 18, 1907 (Meiji 40), it was merged ({{ill|lt=shrine consolidation|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|lt=gōshi|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}) into nearby village-shrine Hachiman Shrine (now {{ill|lt=Tsuboi Hachimangū|Q11429677|WD=Q11429677|1=Tsuboi Hachimangū}}). At the request of former parishioners, it was rebuilt on December 1, 1955 ({{ill|lt=1955|Q18577|WD=Q18577|1=1955}}), borrowing land owned by {{ill|lt=Daikoku-ji (Habikino)|Q17228997|WD=Q17228997|lt=Daikoku-ji Temple|1=Daikoku-ji Temple}}. After the merger, the former shrine site was sold and used for soil extraction, and the hill has nearly disappeared. === References === {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Oookami-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおけおかみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:水神]] [[Category:山王信仰]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * おおけおかみじんじゃ * おおおかみじんじゃ * たけおかみじんじゃ == Commons category (P373) == * Oookami-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府羽曳野市大黒496', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Oookami-jinja}} [[JA:大祁於賀美神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11438318}} == Japanese content == {{Unified character|祁|ネ|示}} {{Shinto shrine | Name = Ōke Okami Shrine | Image = [[File:Oookami-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}} Daikoku 496 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami no Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | Other name = {{ill|lt=Sannō Gongen|Q11470054|WD=Q11470054|1=Sannō Gongen}} | Main hall style = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Annual festival = {{ill|lt=October 17|Q2962|WD=Q2962|1=October 17}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Ōke
Okami Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ōke Okami Shrine''' (Takeokami Jinja / Ōkeokami Jinja / Ōōkami Jinja) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It is also written as “Take Okami Shrine” (太祁於賀美神社), and enshrines {{ill|lt=Okami no Kami|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Takaokami no Kami|1=Kuraokami}} (a water deity). === History === This shrine appears in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as “Ōke Okami Shrine, Ishikawa District, {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}}{{ill|lt=Ishikawa District (Osaka Prefecture)|Q11586399|WD=Q11586399|lt=Ishikawa District|1=Ishikawa district}},” but it is not found in the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=Six National Histories|1=Rikkokushi}}, and its history before the Edo period is unknown. Both the former and current shrine sites were within old {{ill|lt=Furuichi District|Q11411239|WD=Q11411239|1=Furuichi district}}, but that reflects district-boundary changes after the Engishiki period. Originally the shrine stood on a hill summit east of the current site, and until the Edo period it was called {{ill|lt=Sannō Gongen|Q11470054|WD=Q11470054|1=Sannō Gongen}} (even now, parishioners call it “Sannō Gongen”). The 1801 ({{ill|lt=Kyōwa|Q960763|WD=Q960763|1=Kyōwa}} 1) work ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawachi Meisho Zue|1=UNKNOWN}}'' says: “Listed under Ishikawa District in the Engishiki. Now called Sannō and revered as the tutelary deity of this place.” In the early Meiji period it became a village shrine, but on November 18, 1907 (Meiji 40), it was merged ({{ill|lt=shrine consolidation|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|lt=gōshi|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}) into nearby village-shrine Hachiman Shrine (now {{ill|lt=Tsuboi Hachimangū|Q11429677|WD=Q11429677|1=Tsuboi Hachimangū}}). At the request of former parishioners, it was rebuilt on December 1, 1955 ({{ill|lt=1955|Q18577|WD=Q18577|1=1955}}), borrowing land owned by {{ill|lt=Daikoku-ji (Habikino)|Q17228997|WD=Q17228997|lt=Daikoku-ji Temple|1=Daikoku-ji Temple}}. After the merger, the former shrine site was sold and used for soil extraction, and the hill has nearly disappeared. === References === {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Oookami-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおけおかみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:水神]] [[Category:山王信仰]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

category|Category:Oookami-jinja}} [[JA:大祁於賀美神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11438318}} == Japanese content == {{Unified character|祁|ネ|示}} {{Shinto shrine | Name = Ōke Okami Shrine | Image = [[File:Oookami-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}} Daikoku 496 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami no Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | Other name = {{ill|lt=Sannō Gongen|Q11470054|WD=Q11470054|1=Sannō Gongen}} | Main hall style = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Annual festival = {{ill|lt=October 17|Q2962|WD=Q2962|1=October 17}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Ōke
Okami Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ōke Okami Shrine''' (Takeokami Jinja / Ōkeokami Jinja / Ōōkami Jinja) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It is also written as “Take Okami Shrine” (太祁於賀美神社), and enshrines {{ill|lt=Okami no Kami|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Takaokami no Kami|1=Kuraokami}} (a water deity). === History === This shrine appears in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as “Ōke Okami Shrine, Ishikawa District, {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}}{{ill|lt=Ishikawa District (Osaka Prefecture)|Q11586399|WD=Q11586399|lt=Ishikawa District|1=Ishikawa district}},” but it is not found in the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=Six National Histories|1=Rikkokushi}}, and its history before the Edo period is unknown. Both the former and current shrine sites were within old {{ill|lt=Furuichi District|Q11411239|WD=Q11411239|1=Furuichi district}}, but that reflects district-boundary changes after the Engishiki period. Originally the shrine stood on a hill summit east of the current site, and until the Edo period it was called {{ill|lt=Sannō Gongen|Q11470054|WD=Q11470054|1=Sannō Gongen}} (even now, parishioners call it “Sannō Gongen”). The 1801 ({{ill|lt=Kyōwa|Q960763|WD=Q960763|1=Kyōwa}} 1) work ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawachi Meisho Zue|1=UNKNOWN}}'' says: “Listed under Ishikawa District in the Engishiki. Now called Sannō and revered as the tutelary deity of this place.” In the early Meiji period it became a village shrine, but on November 18, 1907 (Meiji 40), it was merged ({{ill|lt=shrine consolidation|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|lt=gōshi|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}) into nearby village-shrine Hachiman Shrine (now {{ill|lt=Tsuboi Hachimangū|Q11429677|WD=Q11429677|1=Tsuboi Hachimangū}}). At the request of former parishioners, it was rebuilt on December 1, 1955 ({{ill|lt=1955|Q18577|WD=Q18577|1=1955}}), borrowing land owned by {{ill|lt=Daikoku-ji (Habikino)|Q17228997|WD=Q17228997|lt=Daikoku-ji Temple|1=Daikoku-ji Temple}}. After the merger, the former shrine site was sold and used for soil extraction, and the hill has nearly disappeared. === References === {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Oookami-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおけおかみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:水神]] [[Category:山王信仰]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * おおけおかみじんじゃ * おおおかみじんじゃ * たけおかみじんじゃ == Commons category (P373) == * Oookami-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府羽曳野市大黒496', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Oookami-jinja}} [[JA:大祁於賀美神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11438318}} == Japanese content == {{Unified character|祁|ネ|示}} {{Shinto shrine | Name = Ōke Okami Shrine | Image = [[File:Oookami-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}} Daikoku 496 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami no Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | Other name = {{ill|lt=Sannō Gongen|Q11470054|WD=Q11470054|1=Sannō Gongen}} | Main hall style = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Annual festival = {{ill|lt=October 17|Q2962|WD=Q2962|1=October 17}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Ōke
Okami Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ōke Okami Shrine''' (Takeokami Jinja / Ōkeokami Jinja / Ōōkami Jinja) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It is also written as “Take Okami Shrine” (太祁於賀美神社), and enshrines {{ill|lt=Okami no Kami|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Takaokami no Kami|1=Kuraokami}} (a water deity). === History === This shrine appears in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as “Ōke Okami Shrine, Ishikawa District, {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}}{{ill|lt=Ishikawa District (Osaka Prefecture)|Q11586399|WD=Q11586399|lt=Ishikawa District|1=Ishikawa district}},” but it is not found in the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=Six National Histories|1=Rikkokushi}}, and its history before the Edo period is unknown. Both the former and current shrine sites were within old {{ill|lt=Furuichi District|Q11411239|WD=Q11411239|1=Furuichi district}}, but that reflects district-boundary changes after the Engishiki period. Originally the shrine stood on a hill summit east of the current site, and until the Edo period it was called {{ill|lt=Sannō Gongen|Q11470054|WD=Q11470054|1=Sannō Gongen}} (even now, parishioners call it “Sannō Gongen”). The 1801 ({{ill|lt=Kyōwa|Q960763|WD=Q960763|1=Kyōwa}} 1) work ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawachi Meisho Zue|1=UNKNOWN}}'' says: “Listed under Ishikawa District in the Engishiki. Now called Sannō and revered as the tutelary deity of this place.” In the early Meiji period it became a village shrine, but on November 18, 1907 (Meiji 40), it was merged ({{ill|lt=shrine consolidation|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|lt=gōshi|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}) into nearby village-shrine Hachiman Shrine (now {{ill|lt=Tsuboi Hachimangū|Q11429677|WD=Q11429677|1=Tsuboi Hachimangū}}). At the request of former parishioners, it was rebuilt on December 1, 1955 ({{ill|lt=1955|Q18577|WD=Q18577|1=1955}}), borrowing land owned by {{ill|lt=Daikoku-ji (Habikino)|Q17228997|WD=Q17228997|lt=Daikoku-ji Temple|1=Daikoku-ji Temple}}. After the merger, the former shrine site was sold and used for soil extraction, and the hill has nearly disappeared. === References === {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Oookami-jinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:水神]] [[Category:山王信仰]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * おおけおかみじんじゃ * おおおかみじんじゃ * たけおかみじんじゃ == Commons category (P373) == * Oookami-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府羽曳野市大黒496', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Oookami-jinja}} [[JA:大祁於賀美神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11438318}} == Japanese content == {{Unified character|祁|ネ|示}} {{Shinto shrine | Name = Ōke Okami Shrine | Image = [[File:Oookami-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}} Daikoku 496 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami no Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | Other name = {{ill|lt=Sannō Gongen|Q11470054|WD=Q11470054|1=Sannō Gongen}} | Main hall style = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Annual festival = {{ill|lt=October 17|Q2962|WD=Q2962|1=October 17}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Ōke
Okami Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ōke Okami Shrine''' (Takeokami Jinja / Ōkeokami Jinja / Ōōkami Jinja) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It is also written as “Take Okami Shrine” (太祁於賀美神社), and enshrines {{ill|lt=Okami no Kami|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Takaokami no Kami|1=Kuraokami}} (a water deity). === History === This shrine appears in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as “Ōke Okami Shrine, Ishikawa District, {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}}{{ill|lt=Ishikawa District (Osaka Prefecture)|Q11586399|WD=Q11586399|lt=Ishikawa District|1=Ishikawa district}},” but it is not found in the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=Six National Histories|1=Rikkokushi}}, and its history before the Edo period is unknown. Both the former and current shrine sites were within old {{ill|lt=Furuichi District|Q11411239|WD=Q11411239|1=Furuichi district}}, but that reflects district-boundary changes after the Engishiki period. Originally the shrine stood on a hill summit east of the current site, and until the Edo period it was called {{ill|lt=Sannō Gongen|Q11470054|WD=Q11470054|1=Sannō Gongen}} (even now, parishioners call it “Sannō Gongen”). The 1801 ({{ill|lt=Kyōwa|Q960763|WD=Q960763|1=Kyōwa}} 1) work ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawachi Meisho Zue|1=UNKNOWN}}'' says: “Listed under Ishikawa District in the Engishiki. Now called Sannō and revered as the tutelary deity of this place.” In the early Meiji period it became a village shrine, but on November 18, 1907 (Meiji 40), it was merged ({{ill|lt=shrine consolidation|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|lt=gōshi|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}) into nearby village-shrine Hachiman Shrine (now {{ill|lt=Tsuboi Hachimangū|Q11429677|WD=Q11429677|1=Tsuboi Hachimangū}}). At the request of former parishioners, it was rebuilt on December 1, 1955 ({{ill|lt=1955|Q18577|WD=Q18577|1=1955}}), borrowing land owned by {{ill|lt=Daikoku-ji (Habikino)|Q17228997|WD=Q17228997|lt=Daikoku-ji Temple|1=Daikoku-ji Temple}}. After the merger, the former shrine site was sold and used for soil extraction, and the hill has nearly disappeared. === References === {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Oookami-jinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:水神]] [[Category:山王信仰]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * おおけおかみじんじゃ * おおおかみじんじゃ * たけおかみじんじゃ == Commons category (P373) == * Oookami-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府羽曳野市大黒496', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Oookami-jinja}} [[JA:大祁於賀美神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11438318}} == Japanese content == {{Unified character|祁|ネ|示}} {{Shinto shrine | Name = Ōke Okami Shrine | Image = [[File:Oookami-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}} Daikoku 496 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami no Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | Other name = {{ill|lt=Sannō Gongen|Q11470054|WD=Q11470054|1=Sannō Gongen}} | Main hall style = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Annual festival = {{ill|lt=October 17|Q2962|WD=Q2962|1=October 17}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Ōke
Okami Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ōke Okami Shrine''' (Takeokami Jinja / Ōkeokami Jinja / Ōōkami Jinja) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It is also written as “Take Okami Shrine” (太祁於賀美神社), and enshrines {{ill|lt=Okami no Kami|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Takaokami no Kami|1=Kuraokami}} (a water deity). === History === This shrine appears in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as “Ōke Okami Shrine, Ishikawa District, {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}}{{ill|lt=Ishikawa District (Osaka Prefecture)|Q11586399|WD=Q11586399|lt=Ishikawa District|1=Ishikawa district}},” but it is not found in the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=Six National Histories|1=Rikkokushi}}, and its history before the Edo period is unknown. Both the former and current shrine sites were within old {{ill|lt=Furuichi District|Q11411239|WD=Q11411239|1=Furuichi district}}, but that reflects district-boundary changes after the Engishiki period. Originally the shrine stood on a hill summit east of the current site, and until the Edo period it was called {{ill|lt=Sannō Gongen|Q11470054|WD=Q11470054|1=Sannō Gongen}} (even now, parishioners call it “Sannō Gongen”). The 1801 ({{ill|lt=Kyōwa|Q960763|WD=Q960763|1=Kyōwa}} 1) work ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawachi Meisho Zue|1=UNKNOWN}}'' says: “Listed under Ishikawa District in the Engishiki. Now called Sannō and revered as the tutelary deity of this place.” In the early Meiji period it became a village shrine, but on November 18, 1907 (Meiji 40), it was merged ({{ill|lt=shrine consolidation|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|lt=gōshi|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}) into nearby village-shrine Hachiman Shrine (now {{ill|lt=Tsuboi Hachimangū|Q11429677|WD=Q11429677|1=Tsuboi Hachimangū}}). At the request of former parishioners, it was rebuilt on December 1, 1955 ({{ill|lt=1955|Q18577|WD=Q18577|1=1955}}), borrowing land owned by {{ill|lt=Daikoku-ji (Habikino)|Q17228997|WD=Q17228997|lt=Daikoku-ji Temple|1=Daikoku-ji Temple}}. After the merger, the former shrine site was sold and used for soil extraction, and the hill has nearly disappeared. === References === {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Oookami-jinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:水神]] [[Category:山王信仰]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * おおけおかみじんじゃ * おおおかみじんじゃ * たけおかみじんじゃ == Commons category (P373) == * Oookami-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府羽曳野市大黒496', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Oookami-jinja}} [[JA:大祁於賀美神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11438318}} == Japanese content == {{Unified character|祁|ネ|示}} {{Shinto shrine | Name = Ōke Okami Shrine | Image = [[File:Oookami-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}} Daikoku 496 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami no Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | Other name = {{ill|lt=Sannō Gongen|Q11470054|WD=Q11470054|1=Sannō Gongen}} | Main hall style = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Annual festival = {{ill|lt=October 17|Q2962|WD=Q2962|1=October 17}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Ōke
Okami Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ōke Okami Shrine''' (Takeokami Jinja / Ōkeokami Jinja / Ōōkami Jinja) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It is also written as “Take Okami Shrine” (太祁於賀美神社), and enshrines {{ill|lt=Okami no Kami|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Takaokami no Kami|1=Kuraokami}} (a water deity). === History === This shrine appears in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as “Ōke Okami Shrine, Ishikawa District, {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}}{{ill|lt=Ishikawa District (Osaka Prefecture)|Q11586399|WD=Q11586399|lt=Ishikawa District|1=Ishikawa district}},” but it is not found in the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=Six National Histories|1=Rikkokushi}}, and its history before the Edo period is unknown. Both the former and current shrine sites were within old {{ill|lt=Furuichi District|Q11411239|WD=Q11411239|1=Furuichi district}}, but that reflects district-boundary changes after the Engishiki period. Originally the shrine stood on a hill summit east of the current site, and until the Edo period it was called {{ill|lt=Sannō Gongen|Q11470054|WD=Q11470054|1=Sannō Gongen}} (even now, parishioners call it “Sannō Gongen”). The 1801 ({{ill|lt=Kyōwa|Q960763|WD=Q960763|1=Kyōwa}} 1) work ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawachi Meisho Zue|1=UNKNOWN}}'' says: “Listed under Ishikawa District in the Engishiki. Now called Sannō and revered as the tutelary deity of this place.” In the early Meiji period it became a village shrine, but on November 18, 1907 (Meiji 40), it was merged ({{ill|lt=shrine consolidation|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|lt=gōshi|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}) into nearby village-shrine Hachiman Shrine (now {{ill|lt=Tsuboi Hachimangū|Q11429677|WD=Q11429677|1=Tsuboi Hachimangū}}). At the request of former parishioners, it was rebuilt on December 1, 1955 ({{ill|lt=1955|Q18577|WD=Q18577|1=1955}}), borrowing land owned by {{ill|lt=Daikoku-ji (Habikino)|Q17228997|WD=Q17228997|lt=Daikoku-ji Temple|1=Daikoku-ji Temple}}. After the merger, the former shrine site was sold and used for soil extraction, and the hill has nearly disappeared. === References === {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Oookami-jinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:水神]] [[Category:Sannō Ichijitsu Shintō]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

category|Category:Oookami-jinja}} [[JA:大祁於賀美神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11438318}} == Japanese content == {{Unified character|祁|ネ|示}} {{Shinto shrine | Name = Ōke Okami Shrine | Image = [[File:Oookami-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}} Daikoku 496 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami no Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | Other name = {{ill|lt=Sannō Gongen|Q11470054|WD=Q11470054|1=Sannō Gongen}} | Main hall style = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Annual festival = {{ill|lt=October 17|Q2962|WD=Q2962|1=October 17}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Ōke
Okami Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ōke Okami Shrine''' (Takeokami Jinja / Ōkeokami Jinja / Ōōkami Jinja) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It is also written as “Take Okami Shrine” (太祁於賀美神社), and enshrines {{ill|lt=Okami no Kami|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Takaokami no Kami|1=Kuraokami}} (a water deity). === History === This shrine appears in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as “Ōke Okami Shrine, Ishikawa District, {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}}{{ill|lt=Ishikawa District (Osaka Prefecture)|Q11586399|WD=Q11586399|lt=Ishikawa District|1=Ishikawa district}},” but it is not found in the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=Six National Histories|1=Rikkokushi}}, and its history before the Edo period is unknown. Both the former and current shrine sites were within old {{ill|lt=Furuichi District|Q11411239|WD=Q11411239|1=Furuichi district}}, but that reflects district-boundary changes after the Engishiki period. Originally the shrine stood on a hill summit east of the current site, and until the Edo period it was called {{ill|lt=Sannō Gongen|Q11470054|WD=Q11470054|1=Sannō Gongen}} (even now, parishioners call it “Sannō Gongen”). The 1801 ({{ill|lt=Kyōwa|Q960763|WD=Q960763|1=Kyōwa}} 1) work ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawachi Meisho Zue|1=UNKNOWN}}'' says: “Listed under Ishikawa District in the Engishiki. Now called Sannō and revered as the tutelary deity of this place.” In the early Meiji period it became a village shrine, but on November 18, 1907 (Meiji 40), it was merged ({{ill|lt=shrine consolidation|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|lt=gōshi|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}) into nearby village-shrine Hachiman Shrine (now {{ill|lt=Tsuboi Hachimangū|Q11429677|WD=Q11429677|1=Tsuboi Hachimangū}}). At the request of former parishioners, it was rebuilt on December 1, 1955 ({{ill|lt=1955|Q18577|WD=Q18577|1=1955}}), borrowing land owned by {{ill|lt=Daikoku-ji (Habikino)|Q17228997|WD=Q17228997|lt=Daikoku-ji Temple|1=Daikoku-ji Temple}}. After the merger, the former shrine site was sold and used for soil extraction, and the hill has nearly disappeared. === References === {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Oookami-jinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Water deities]] [[Category:Sannō Ichijitsu Shintō]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * おおけおかみじんじゃ * おおおかみじんじゃ * たけおかみじんじゃ == Commons category (P373) == * Oookami-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府羽曳野市大黒496', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Oookami-jinja}} [[JA:大祁於賀美神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11438318}} == Japanese content == {{Unified character|祁|ネ|示}} {{Shinto shrine | Name = Ōke Okami Shrine | Image = [[File:Oookami-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}} Daikoku 496 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami no Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | Other name = {{ill|lt=Sannō Gongen|Q11470054|WD=Q11470054|1=Sannō Gongen}} | Main hall style = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Annual festival = {{ill|lt=October 17|Q2962|WD=Q2962|1=October 17}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Ōke
Okami Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ōke Okami Shrine''' (Takeokami Jinja / Ōkeokami Jinja / Ōōkami Jinja) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It is also written as “Take Okami Shrine” (太祁於賀美神社), and enshrines {{ill|lt=Okami no Kami|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Takaokami no Kami|1=Kuraokami}} (a water deity). === History === This shrine appears in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as “Ōke Okami Shrine, Ishikawa District, {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}}{{ill|lt=Ishikawa District (Osaka Prefecture)|Q11586399|WD=Q11586399|lt=Ishikawa District|1=Ishikawa district}},” but it is not found in the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=Six National Histories|1=Rikkokushi}}, and its history before the Edo period is unknown. Both the former and current shrine sites were within old {{ill|lt=Furuichi District|Q11411239|WD=Q11411239|1=Furuichi district}}, but that reflects district-boundary changes after the Engishiki period. Originally the shrine stood on a hill summit east of the current site, and until the Edo period it was called {{ill|lt=Sannō Gongen|Q11470054|WD=Q11470054|1=Sannō Gongen}} (even now, parishioners call it “Sannō Gongen”). The 1801 ({{ill|lt=Kyōwa|Q960763|WD=Q960763|1=Kyōwa}} 1) work ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawachi Meisho Zue|1=UNKNOWN}}'' says: “Listed under Ishikawa District in the Engishiki. Now called Sannō and revered as the tutelary deity of this place.” In the early Meiji period it became a village shrine, but on November 18, 1907 (Meiji 40), it was merged ({{ill|lt=shrine consolidation|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|lt=gōshi|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}) into nearby village-shrine Hachiman Shrine (now {{ill|lt=Tsuboi Hachimangū|Q11429677|WD=Q11429677|1=Tsuboi Hachimangū}}). At the request of former parishioners, it was rebuilt on December 1, 1955 ({{ill|lt=1955|Q18577|WD=Q18577|1=1955}}), borrowing land owned by {{ill|lt=Daikoku-ji (Habikino)|Q17228997|WD=Q17228997|lt=Daikoku-ji Temple|1=Daikoku-ji Temple}}. After the merger, the former shrine site was sold and used for soil extraction, and the hill has nearly disappeared. === References === {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Oookami-jinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Water deities]] [[Category:Sannō Ichijitsu Shintō]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

category|Category:Oookami-jinja}} [[JA:大祁於賀美神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11438318}} == Japanese content == {{Unified character|祁|ネ|示}} {{Shinto shrine | Name = Ōke Okami Shrine | Image = [[File:Oookami-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}} Daikoku 496 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami no Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | Other name = {{ill|lt=Sannō Gongen|Q11470054|WD=Q11470054|1=Sannō Gongen}} | Main hall style = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Annual festival = {{ill|lt=October 17|Q2962|WD=Q2962|1=October 17}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Ōke
Okami Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ōke Okami Shrine''' (Takeokami Jinja / Ōkeokami Jinja / Ōōkami Jinja) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It is also written as “Take Okami Shrine” (太祁於賀美神社), and enshrines {{ill|lt=Okami no Kami|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Takaokami no Kami|1=Kuraokami}} (a water deity). === History === This shrine appears in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as “Ōke Okami Shrine, Ishikawa District, {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}}{{ill|lt=Ishikawa District (Osaka Prefecture)|Q11586399|WD=Q11586399|lt=Ishikawa District|1=Ishikawa district}},” but it is not found in the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=Six National Histories|1=Rikkokushi}}, and its history before the Edo period is unknown. Both the former and current shrine sites were within old {{ill|lt=Furuichi District|Q11411239|WD=Q11411239|1=Furuichi district}}, but that reflects district-boundary changes after the Engishiki period. Originally the shrine stood on a hill summit east of the current site, and until the Edo period it was called {{ill|lt=Sannō Gongen|Q11470054|WD=Q11470054|1=Sannō Gongen}} (even now, parishioners call it “Sannō Gongen”). The 1801 ({{ill|lt=Kyōwa|Q960763|WD=Q960763|1=Kyōwa}} 1) work ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawachi Meisho Zue|1=UNKNOWN}}'' says: “Listed under Ishikawa District in the Engishiki. Now called Sannō and revered as the tutelary deity of this place.” In the early Meiji period it became a village shrine, but on November 18, 1907 (Meiji 40), it was merged ({{ill|lt=shrine consolidation|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|lt=gōshi|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}) into nearby village-shrine Hachiman Shrine (now {{ill|lt=Tsuboi Hachimangū|Q11429677|WD=Q11429677|1=Tsuboi Hachimangū}}). At the request of former parishioners, it was rebuilt on December 1, 1955 ({{ill|lt=1955|Q18577|WD=Q18577|1=1955}}), borrowing land owned by {{ill|lt=Daikoku-ji (Habikino)|Q17228997|WD=Q17228997|lt=Daikoku-ji Temple|1=Daikoku-ji Temple}}. After the merger, the former shrine site was sold and used for soil extraction, and the hill has nearly disappeared. === References === {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Oookami-jinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Water deities]] [[Category:Sannō Ichijitsu Shintō]] [[Category:Buildings in Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * おおけおかみじんじゃ * おおおかみじんじゃ * たけおかみじんじゃ == Commons category (P373) == * Oookami-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府羽曳野市大黒496', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Oookami-jinja}} [[JA:大祁於賀美神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11438318}} == Japanese content == {{Unified character|祁|ネ|示}} {{Shinto shrine | Name = Ōke Okami Shrine | Image = [[File:Oookami-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}} Daikoku 496 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami no Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | Other name = {{ill|lt=Sannō Gongen|Q11470054|WD=Q11470054|1=Sannō Gongen}} | Main hall style = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Annual festival = {{ill|lt=October 17|Q2962|WD=Q2962|1=October 17}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Ōke
Okami Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ōke Okami Shrine''' (Takeokami Jinja / Ōkeokami Jinja / Ōōkami Jinja) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It is also written as “Take Okami Shrine” (太祁於賀美神社), and enshrines {{ill|lt=Okami no Kami|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Takaokami no Kami|1=Kuraokami}} (a water deity). === History === This shrine appears in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as “Ōke Okami Shrine, Ishikawa District, {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}}{{ill|lt=Ishikawa District (Osaka Prefecture)|Q11586399|WD=Q11586399|lt=Ishikawa District|1=Ishikawa district}},” but it is not found in the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=Six National Histories|1=Rikkokushi}}, and its history before the Edo period is unknown. Both the former and current shrine sites were within old {{ill|lt=Furuichi District|Q11411239|WD=Q11411239|1=Furuichi district}}, but that reflects district-boundary changes after the Engishiki period. Originally the shrine stood on a hill summit east of the current site, and until the Edo period it was called {{ill|lt=Sannō Gongen|Q11470054|WD=Q11470054|1=Sannō Gongen}} (even now, parishioners call it “Sannō Gongen”). The 1801 ({{ill|lt=Kyōwa|Q960763|WD=Q960763|1=Kyōwa}} 1) work ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawachi Meisho Zue|1=UNKNOWN}}'' says: “Listed under Ishikawa District in the Engishiki. Now called Sannō and revered as the tutelary deity of this place.” In the early Meiji period it became a village shrine, but on November 18, 1907 (Meiji 40), it was merged ({{ill|lt=shrine consolidation|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|lt=gōshi|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}) into nearby village-shrine Hachiman Shrine (now {{ill|lt=Tsuboi Hachimangū|Q11429677|WD=Q11429677|1=Tsuboi Hachimangū}}). At the request of former parishioners, it was rebuilt on December 1, 1955 ({{ill|lt=1955|Q18577|WD=Q18577|1=1955}}), borrowing land owned by {{ill|lt=Daikoku-ji (Habikino)|Q17228997|WD=Q17228997|lt=Daikoku-ji Temple|1=Daikoku-ji Temple}}. After the merger, the former shrine site was sold and used for soil extraction, and the hill has nearly disappeared. === References === {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Oookami-jinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Water deities]] [[Category:Sannō Ichijitsu Shintō]] [[Category:Buildings in Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

category|Category:Oookami-jinja}} [[JA:大祁於賀美神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11438318}} == Japanese content == {{Unified character|祁|ネ|示}} {{Shinto shrine | Name = Ōke Okami Shrine | Image = [[File:Oookami-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}} Daikoku 496 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami no Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | Other name = {{ill|lt=Sannō Gongen|Q11470054|WD=Q11470054|1=Sannō Gongen}} | Main hall style = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Annual festival = {{ill|lt=October 17|Q2962|WD=Q2962|1=October 17}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Ōke
Okami Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ōke Okami Shrine''' (Takeokami Jinja / Ōkeokami Jinja / Ōōkami Jinja) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It is also written as “Take Okami Shrine” (太祁於賀美神社), and enshrines {{ill|lt=Okami no Kami|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Takaokami no Kami|1=Kuraokami}} (a water deity). === History === This shrine appears in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as “Ōke Okami Shrine, Ishikawa District, {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}}{{ill|lt=Ishikawa District (Osaka Prefecture)|Q11586399|WD=Q11586399|lt=Ishikawa District|1=Ishikawa district}},” but it is not found in the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=Six National Histories|1=Rikkokushi}}, and its history before the Edo period is unknown. Both the former and current shrine sites were within old {{ill|lt=Furuichi District|Q11411239|WD=Q11411239|1=Furuichi district}}, but that reflects district-boundary changes after the Engishiki period. Originally the shrine stood on a hill summit east of the current site, and until the Edo period it was called {{ill|lt=Sannō Gongen|Q11470054|WD=Q11470054|1=Sannō Gongen}} (even now, parishioners call it “Sannō Gongen”). The 1801 ({{ill|lt=Kyōwa|Q960763|WD=Q960763|1=Kyōwa}} 1) work ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawachi Meisho Zue|1=UNKNOWN}}'' says: “Listed under Ishikawa District in the Engishiki. Now called Sannō and revered as the tutelary deity of this place.” In the early Meiji period it became a village shrine, but on November 18, 1907 (Meiji 40), it was merged ({{ill|lt=shrine consolidation|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|lt=gōshi|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}) into nearby village-shrine Hachiman Shrine (now {{ill|lt=Tsuboi Hachimangū|Q11429677|WD=Q11429677|1=Tsuboi Hachimangū}}). At the request of former parishioners, it was rebuilt on December 1, 1955 ({{ill|lt=1955|Q18577|WD=Q18577|1=1955}}), borrowing land owned by {{ill|lt=Daikoku-ji (Habikino)|Q17228997|WD=Q17228997|lt=Daikoku-ji Temple|1=Daikoku-ji Temple}}. After the merger, the former shrine site was sold and used for soil extraction, and the hill has nearly disappeared. === References === {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Oookami-jinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Water deities]] [[Category:Sannō Ichijitsu Shintō]] [[Category:Buildings in Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * おおけおかみじんじゃ * おおおかみじんじゃ * たけおかみじんじゃ == Commons category (P373) == * Oookami-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府羽曳野市大黒496', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Oookami-jinja}} [[JA:大祁於賀美神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11438318}} == Japanese content == {{Unified character|祁|ネ|示}} {{Shinto shrine | Name = Ōke Okami Shrine | Image = [[File:Oookami-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}} Daikoku 496 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami no Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | Other name = {{ill|lt=Sannō Gongen|Q11470054|WD=Q11470054|1=Sannō Gongen}} | Main hall style = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Annual festival = {{ill|lt=October 17|Q2962|WD=Q2962|1=October 17}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Ōke
Okami Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ōke Okami Shrine''' (Takeokami Jinja / Ōkeokami Jinja / Ōōkami Jinja) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It is also written as “Take Okami Shrine” (太祁於賀美神社), and enshrines {{ill|lt=Okami no Kami|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Takaokami no Kami|1=Kuraokami}} (a water deity). === History === This shrine appears in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as “Ōke Okami Shrine, Ishikawa District, {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}}{{ill|lt=Ishikawa District (Osaka Prefecture)|Q11586399|WD=Q11586399|lt=Ishikawa District|1=Ishikawa district}},” but it is not found in the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=Six National Histories|1=Rikkokushi}}, and its history before the Edo period is unknown. Both the former and current shrine sites were within old {{ill|lt=Furuichi District|Q11411239|WD=Q11411239|1=Furuichi district}}, but that reflects district-boundary changes after the Engishiki period. Originally the shrine stood on a hill summit east of the current site, and until the Edo period it was called {{ill|lt=Sannō Gongen|Q11470054|WD=Q11470054|1=Sannō Gongen}} (even now, parishioners call it “Sannō Gongen”). The 1801 ({{ill|lt=Kyōwa|Q960763|WD=Q960763|1=Kyōwa}} 1) work ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawachi Meisho Zue|1=UNKNOWN}}'' says: “Listed under Ishikawa District in the Engishiki. Now called Sannō and revered as the tutelary deity of this place.” In the early Meiji period it became a village shrine, but on November 18, 1907 (Meiji 40), it was merged ({{ill|lt=shrine consolidation|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|lt=gōshi|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}) into nearby village-shrine Hachiman Shrine (now {{ill|lt=Tsuboi Hachimangū|Q11429677|WD=Q11429677|1=Tsuboi Hachimangū}}). At the request of former parishioners, it was rebuilt on December 1, 1955 ({{ill|lt=1955|Q18577|WD=Q18577|1=1955}}), borrowing land owned by {{ill|lt=Daikoku-ji (Habikino)|Q17228997|WD=Q17228997|lt=Daikoku-ji Temple|1=Daikoku-ji Temple}}. After the merger, the former shrine site was sold and used for soil extraction, and the hill has nearly disappeared. === References === {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Oookami-jinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Water deities]] [[Category:Sannō Ichijitsu Shintō]] [[Category:Buildings in Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * おおけおかみじんじゃ * おおおかみじんじゃ * たけおかみじんじゃ == Commons category (P373) == * Oookami-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府羽曳野市大黒496', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Oookami-jinja}} [[JA:大祁於賀美神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11438318}} == Japanese content == {{Unified character|祁|ネ|示}} {{Shinto shrine | Name = Ōke Okami Shrine | Image = [[File:Oookami-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}} Daikoku 496 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami no Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | Other name = {{ill|lt=Sannō Gongen|Q11470054|WD=Q11470054|1=Sannō Gongen}} | Main hall style = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Annual festival = {{ill|lt=October 17|Q2962|WD=Q2962|1=October 17}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Ōke
Okami Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ōke Okami Shrine''' (Takeokami Jinja / Ōkeokami Jinja / Ōōkami Jinja) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It is also written as “Take Okami Shrine” (太祁於賀美神社), and enshrines {{ill|lt=Okami no Kami|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Takaokami no Kami|1=Kuraokami}} (a water deity). === History === This shrine appears in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as “Ōke Okami Shrine, Ishikawa District, {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}}{{ill|lt=Ishikawa District (Osaka Prefecture)|Q11586399|WD=Q11586399|lt=Ishikawa District|1=Ishikawa district}},” but it is not found in the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=Six National Histories|1=Rikkokushi}}, and its history before the Edo period is unknown. Both the former and current shrine sites were within old {{ill|lt=Furuichi District|Q11411239|WD=Q11411239|1=Furuichi district}}, but that reflects district-boundary changes after the Engishiki period. Originally the shrine stood on a hill summit east of the current site, and until the Edo period it was called {{ill|lt=Sannō Gongen|Q11470054|WD=Q11470054|1=Sannō Gongen}} (even now, parishioners call it “Sannō Gongen”). The 1801 ({{ill|lt=Kyōwa|Q960763|WD=Q960763|1=Kyōwa}} 1) work ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawachi Meisho Zue|1=UNKNOWN}}'' says: “Listed under Ishikawa District in the Engishiki. Now called Sannō and revered as the tutelary deity of this place.” In the early Meiji period it became a village shrine, but on November 18, 1907 (Meiji 40), it was merged ({{ill|lt=shrine consolidation|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|lt=gōshi|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}) into nearby village-shrine Hachiman Shrine (now {{ill|lt=Tsuboi Hachimangū|Q11429677|WD=Q11429677|1=Tsuboi Hachimangū}}). At the request of former parishioners, it was rebuilt on December 1, 1955 ({{ill|lt=1955|Q18577|WD=Q18577|1=1955}}), borrowing land owned by {{ill|lt=Daikoku-ji (Habikino)|Q17228997|WD=Q17228997|lt=Daikoku-ji Temple|1=Daikoku-ji Temple}}. After the merger, the former shrine site was sold and used for soil extraction, and the hill has nearly disappeared. === References === {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Oookami-jinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Water deities]] [[Category:Sannō Ichijitsu Shintō]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]]

category|Category:Oookami-jinja}} [[JA:大祁於賀美神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11438318}} == Japanese content == {{Unified character|祁|ネ|示}} {{Shinto shrine | Name = Ōke Okami Shrine | Image = [[File:Oookami-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}} Daikoku 496 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami no Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | Other name = {{ill|lt=Sannō Gongen|Q11470054|WD=Q11470054|1=Sannō Gongen}} | Main hall style = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Annual festival = {{ill|lt=October 17|Q2962|WD=Q2962|1=October 17}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Ōke
Okami Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ōke Okami Shrine''' (Takeokami Jinja / Ōkeokami Jinja / Ōōkami Jinja) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Habikino|Q694308|WD=Q694308|1=Habikino}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It is also written as “Take Okami Shrine” (太祁於賀美神社), and enshrines {{ill|lt=Okami no Kami|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Takaokami no Kami|1=Kuraokami}} (a water deity). === History === This shrine appears in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as “Ōke Okami Shrine, Ishikawa District, {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}}{{ill|lt=Ishikawa District (Osaka Prefecture)|Q11586399|WD=Q11586399|lt=Ishikawa District|1=Ishikawa district}},” but it is not found in the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=Six National Histories|1=Rikkokushi}}, and its history before the Edo period is unknown. Both the former and current shrine sites were within old {{ill|lt=Furuichi District|Q11411239|WD=Q11411239|1=Furuichi district}}, but that reflects district-boundary changes after the Engishiki period. Originally the shrine stood on a hill summit east of the current site, and until the Edo period it was called {{ill|lt=Sannō Gongen|Q11470054|WD=Q11470054|1=Sannō Gongen}} (even now, parishioners call it “Sannō Gongen”). The 1801 ({{ill|lt=Kyōwa|Q960763|WD=Q960763|1=Kyōwa}} 1) work ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawachi Meisho Zue|1=UNKNOWN}}'' says: “Listed under Ishikawa District in the Engishiki. Now called Sannō and revered as the tutelary deity of this place.” In the early Meiji period it became a village shrine, but on November 18, 1907 (Meiji 40), it was merged ({{ill|lt=shrine consolidation|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|lt=gōshi|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}) into nearby village-shrine Hachiman Shrine (now {{ill|lt=Tsuboi Hachimangū|Q11429677|WD=Q11429677|1=Tsuboi Hachimangū}}). At the request of former parishioners, it was rebuilt on December 1, 1955 ({{ill|lt=1955|Q18577|WD=Q18577|1=1955}}), borrowing land owned by {{ill|lt=Daikoku-ji (Habikino)|Q17228997|WD=Q17228997|lt=Daikoku-ji Temple|1=Daikoku-ji Temple}}. After the merger, the former shrine site was sold and used for soil extraction, and the hill has nearly disappeared. === References === {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Oookami-jinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takeokami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Water deities]] [[Category:Sannō Ichijitsu Shintō]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]]
